[[WP:BOTREQ#転載疑い貼り付け]] notice対応
初版に著作権侵害の可能性があるため、暫定的に初版記載事項を編集除去して削除依頼
revision.text:
<!--削除についての議論が終了するまで、下記のメッセージ部分は除去しないでください。もしあなたがこのテンプレートを除去した場合、差し戻されます。またページが保護されることもあります。--> {{Sakujo/本体|2010年9月4日|YOSHI438およびYOSHI438/同一人物と思われる利用者による新規作成記事2}} <!-- 削除についての議論が終了するまで、上記部分は削除しないでください。 --> {{Copyrights}} '''明法博士'''（みょうほうはかせ）は、古代日本の[[律令制]]下において[[大学寮]]に属した官職の一つ。[[令外官]]。定員2名で、当初は[[正七位下]]相当。 後に名門出身者で占められるようになった。
[[神亀]]5年[[7月21日 (旧暦)|7月21日]]（[[728年]][[8月30日]]）の[[格式|格]]において[[文章博士]]とともに設置された<ref>『[[続日本紀]]』[[大宝_(日本)|大宝]]元年8月戊申（8日）条に[[西海道]]を除く各道に明法博士を派遣して[[大宝律令|大宝令]]について説明させたとする記事がある。これについて、[[飛鳥浄御原令]]期には明法博士が置かれていたが大宝令では設置されなかったとする説と大宝令編纂に関わった「令官」のこととする説、明法を原義のように“法律に明るい”と解して単に「法律に通じた学者」とする説がある。（久木幸男『日本古代学校の研究』（1990年、玉川大学出版部）ISBN 4-4720-7981-X）</ref>。当初の名称は'''律学博士'''（りつがくはかせ）であったが、[[天平]]2年[[3月27日 (旧暦)|3月27日]]（[[730年]][[4月18日]]）に'''[[明法生]]'''が設置されてから遠くない時期に'''明法博士'''と改称されたとされている。明法博士の下には'''明法得業生'''（みょうほうとくぎょうしょう）2名と'''明法生'''（みょうほうしょう）10名（後に20名）があった。後には、[[陣定]]などの朝議に際して法律的な見解を記した[[明法勘文]]を作成・提出することも重要な職務となった。平安時代中期には[[讃岐氏]]や[[惟宗氏]]の世襲の傾向が見られたが、中世以降には両氏に代わって、名望の[[坂上氏]]及び[[中原氏]]の世襲となった<ref>[[建久]]2年（[[1191年]]）に公家出身の[[鎌倉幕府]][[政所|政所別当]][[大江広元|中原広元]]が明法博士になっているが、[[明経道]]系の中原氏出身（後に[[大江氏]]に改姓）の広元の就任には、[[土御門通親]]の推挙があったとされるものの、[[九条兼実]]らの批判（『[[玉葉]]』）や[[源頼朝]]の「[[御家人]]が勝手に[[京官]]を兼任してはならない」とする規則に抵触する恐れも指摘されて半年で辞任している。</ref>。
== 脚注 == <references /> == 関連項目 == *[[大学寮]] *[[明法道]] *[[律令制]] *[[律令法]] *[[律令]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:みようほうはかせ}} [[Category:日本の律令制]] [[Category:令外官]] [[Category:大学寮]] [[Category:日本の法学史]]

'''明法博士'''（みょうほうはかせ）は、古代日本の[[律令制]]下において[[大学寮]]に属した官職の一つ。[[令外官]]。定員2名で、当初は[[正七位下]]相当。 後に名門出身者で占められるようになった。
[[神亀]]5年[[7月21日 (旧暦)|7月21日]]（[[728年]][[8月30日]]）の[[格式|格]]において[[文章博士]]とともに設置された<ref>『[[続日本紀]]』[[大宝_(日本)|大宝]]元年8月戊申（8日）条に[[西海道]]を除く各道に明法博士を派遣して[[大宝律令|大宝令]]について説明させたとする記事がある。これについて、[[飛鳥浄御原令]]期には明法博士が置かれていたが大宝令では設置されなかったとする説と大宝令編纂に関わった「令官」のこととする説、明法を原義のように“法律に明るい”と解して単に「法律に通じた学者」とする説がある。（久木幸男『日本古代学校の研究』（1990年、玉川大学出版部）ISBN 4-4720-7981-X）</ref>。当初の名称は'''律学博士'''（りつがくはかせ）であったが、[[天平]]2年[[3月27日 (旧暦)|3月27日]]（[[730年]][[4月18日]]）に'''[[明法生]]'''が設置されてから遠くない時期に'''明法博士'''と改称されたとされている。明法博士の下には'''明法得業生'''（みょうほうとくぎょうしょう）2名と'''明法生'''（みょうほうしょう）10名（後に20名）があった。後には、[[陣定]]などの朝議に際して法律的な見解を記した[[明法勘文]]を作成・提出することも重要な職務となった。平安時代中期には[[讃岐氏]]や[[惟宗氏]]の世襲の傾向が見られたが、中世以降には両氏に代わって、名望の[[坂上氏]]及び[[中原氏]]の世襲となった<ref>[[建久]]2年（[[1191年]]）に公家出身の[[鎌倉幕府]][[政所|政所別当]][[大江広元|中原広元]]が明法博士になっているが、[[明経道]]系の中原氏出身（後に[[大江氏]]に改姓）の広元の就任には、[[土御門通親]]の推挙があったとされるものの、[[九条兼実]]らの批判（『[[玉葉]]』）や[[源頼朝]]の「[[御家人]]が勝手に[[京官]]を兼任してはならない」とする規則に抵触する恐れも指摘されて半年で辞任している。</ref>。
== 脚注 == <references /> == 関連項目 == *[[大学寮]] *[[明法道]] *[[律令制]] *[[律令法]] *[[律令]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:みようほうはかせ}} [[Category:日本の律令制]] [[Category:令外官]] [[Category:大学寮]] [[Category:日本の法学史]]

'''明法博士'''（みょうほうはかせ）は、古代日本の[[律令制]]下において[[大学寮]]に属した官職の一つ。[[令外官]]。定員2名で、当初は[[従六位#正七位|正七位下]]相当。 後に名門出身者で占められるようになった。
[[神亀]]5年[[7月21日 (旧暦)|7月21日]]（[[728年]][[8月30日]]）の[[格式|格]]において[[文章博士]]とともに設置された<ref>『[[続日本紀]]』[[大宝_(日本)|大宝]]元年8月戊申（8日）条に[[西海道]]を除く各道に明法博士を派遣して[[大宝律令|大宝令]]について説明させたとする記事がある。これについて、[[飛鳥浄御原令]]期には明法博士が置かれていたが大宝令では設置されなかったとする説と大宝令編纂に関わった「令官」のこととする説、明法を原義のように“法律に明るい”と解して単に「法律に通じた学者」とする説がある。（久木幸男『日本古代学校の研究』（[[1990年]]、[[玉川大学]]出版部）ISBN 4-4720-7981-X）</ref>。当初の名称は'''律学博士'''（りつがくはかせ）であったが、[[天平]]2年[[3月27日 (旧暦)|3月27日]]（[[730年]][[4月18日]]）に'''[[明法生]]'''が設置されてから遠くない時期に'''明法博士'''と改称されたとされている。明法博士の下には'''明法得業生'''（みょうほうとくぎょうしょう）2名と'''明法生'''（みょうほうしょう）10名（後に20名）があった。後には、[[陣定]]などの朝議に際して法律的な見解を記した[[明法勘文]]を作成・提出することも重要な職務となった。[[平安時代]]中期には[[讃岐氏]]や[[惟宗氏]]の世襲の傾向が見られたが、中世以降には両氏に代わって、名望の[[坂上氏]]及び[[中原氏]]の世襲となった<ref>[[建久]]2年（[[1191年]]）に[[公家]]出身の[[鎌倉幕府]][[政所|政所別当]][[大江広元|中原広元]]が明法博士になっているが、[[明経道]]系の中原氏出身（後に[[大江氏]]に改姓）の広元の就任には、[[源通親|土御門通親(源通親)]]の推挙があったとされるものの、[[九条兼実]]らの批判（『[[玉葉]]』）や[[源頼朝]]の「[[御家人]]が勝手に[[京官]]を兼任してはならない」とする規則に抵触する恐れも指摘されて半年で辞任している。</ref>。

{{DEFAULTSORT:みようほうはかせ}} [[Category:日本の律令制]] [[Category:令外官]] [[Category:大学寮]] [[Category:日本の法学史]]

'''明法博士'''（みょうほうはかせ）は、古代日本の[[律令制]]下において[[大学寮]]に属した[[官職]]の一つ。[[令外官]]。定員2名で、当初は[[従六位#正七位|正七位下]]相当。 後に名門出身者で占められるようになった。
[[神亀]]5年[[7月21日 (旧暦)|7月21日]]（[[728年]][[8月30日]]）の[[格式|格]]において[[文章博士]]とともに設置された<ref>『[[続日本紀]]』[[大宝_(日本)|大宝]]元年8月戊申（8日）条に[[西海道]]を除く各道に明法博士を派遣して[[大宝律令|大宝令]]について説明させたとする記事がある。これについて、[[飛鳥浄御原令]]期には明法博士が置かれていたが大宝令では設置されなかったとする説と大宝令編纂に関わった「令官」のこととする説、明法を原義のように“法律に明るい”と解して単に「法律に通じた学者」とする説がある。（久木幸男『日本古代学校の研究』（[[1990年]]、[[玉川大学]]出版部）ISBN 4-4720-7981-X）</ref>。当初の名称は'''律学博士'''（りつがくはかせ）であったが、[[天平]]2年[[3月27日 (旧暦)|3月27日]]（[[730年]][[4月18日]]）に'''[[明法道|明法生]]'''が設置されてから遠くない時期に'''明法博士'''と改称されたとされている。明法博士の下には'''明法得業生'''（みょうほうとくぎょうしょう）2名と'''明法生'''（みょうほうしょう）10名（後に20名）があった。後には、[[陣定]]などの朝議に際して法律的な見解を記した[[明法勘文]]を作成・提出することも重要な職務となった。[[平安時代]]中期には[[讃岐氏]]や[[惟宗氏]]の世襲の傾向が見られたが、中世以降には両氏に代わって、名望の[[坂上氏]]及び[[中原氏]]の世襲となった<ref>[[建久]]2年（[[1191年]]）に[[公家]]出身の[[鎌倉幕府]][[政所|政所別当]][[大江広元|中原広元]]が明法博士になっているが、[[明経道]]系の中原氏出身（後に[[大江氏]]に改姓）の広元の就任には、[[源通親|土御門通親(源通親)]]の推挙があったとされるものの、[[九条兼実]]らの批判（『[[玉葉]]』）や[[源頼朝]]の「[[御家人]]が勝手に[[京官]]を兼任してはならない」とする規則に抵触する恐れも指摘されて半年で辞任している。</ref>。

{{DEFAULTSORT:みようほうはかせ}} [[Category:日本の律令制]] [[Category:令外官]] [[Category:大学寮]] [[Category:日本の法学史]]

'''明法博士'''（みょうほうはかせ）は、古代日本の[[律令制]]下において[[大学寮]]に属した[[官職]]の一つ。[[令外官]]。定員2名で、当初は[[従六位#正七位|正七位下]]相当。 後に名門出身者で占められるようになった。
[[神亀]]5年[[7月21日 (旧暦)|7月21日]]（[[728年]][[8月30日]]）の[[格式|格]]において[[文章博士]]とともに設置された<ref>『[[続日本紀]]』[[大宝_(日本)|大宝]]元年8月戊申（8日）条に[[西海道]]を除く各道に明法博士を派遣して[[大宝律令|大宝令]]について説明させたとする記事がある。これについて、[[飛鳥浄御原令]]期には明法博士が置かれていたが大宝令では設置されなかったとする説と大宝令編纂に関わった「令官」のこととする説、明法を原義のように“法律に明るい”と解して単に「法律に通じた学者」とする説がある。（久木幸男『日本古代学校の研究』（[[1990年]]、[[玉川大学]]出版部）ISBN 4-4720-7981-X）</ref>。当初の名称は'''律学博士'''（りつがくはかせ）であったが、[[天平]]2年[[3月27日 (旧暦)|3月27日]]（[[730年]][[4月18日]]）に'''[[明法道|明法生]]'''が設置されてから遠くない時期に'''明法博士'''と改称されたとされている。明法博士の下には'''明法得業生'''（みょうほうとくぎょうしょう）2名と'''明法生'''（みょうほうしょう）10名（後に20名）があった。後には、[[陣定]]などの朝議に際して法律的な見解を記した[[明法勘文]]を作成・提出することも重要な職務となった。[[平安時代]]中期には[[讃岐氏]]や[[惟宗氏]]の世襲の傾向が見られたが、中世以降には両氏に代わって、名望の[[坂上氏]]及び[[中原氏]]の世襲となった<ref>[[建久]]2年（[[1191年]]）4月1日に[[鎌倉幕府]][[政所|政所別当]]・[[大江広元|中原広元]]が[[源通親|土御門通親]]の推挙により明法博士となった。この人事について[[九条兼実]]は[[明経道]]系の中原氏出身（後に[[大江氏]]に改姓）である広元の就任は先例に反すると激しく非難している（『[[玉葉]]』4月1日条）。もっとも広元の在任期間は短く、同年11月5日に辞任している（『[[吾妻鏡]]』建久3年3月2日条）。この辞任については、自由任官による[[源頼朝]]の不快が原因とする解釈が一般的であるが、頼朝が在京武力掌握のために検非違使庁を幕府の管理下に置く構想を抱き、検非違使庁の法曹部門を担当する明法博士に広元を就任させたものの、[[建久二年の強訴]]を防げなかった責任を取って辞任したのではないかとする見解もある（佐伯智広「一条能保と鎌倉初期公武関係」『古代文化』564、2006年）。</ref>。
== 脚注 == <references />
== 関連項目 == * [[大学寮]] * [[明法道]] * [[律令制]] * [[律令法]] * [[律令]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:みようほうはかせ}} [[Category:日本の律令制]] [[Category:令外官]] [[Category:大学寮]] [[Category:日本の法学史]]

'''明法博士'''（みょうほうはかせ）は、古代日本の[[律令制]]下において[[大学寮]]に属した[[官職]]の一つ。[[令外官]]。定員2名で、当初は[[従六位#正七位|正七位下]]相当。 後に名門出身者で占められるようになった。
[[神亀]]5年[[7月21日 (旧暦)|7月21日]]（[[728年]][[8月30日]]）の[[格式|格]]において[[文章博士]]とともに設置された<ref>『[[続日本紀]]』[[大宝_(日本)|大宝]]元年8月戊申（8日）条に[[西海道]]を除く各道に明法博士を派遣して[[大宝律令|大宝令]]について説明させたとする記事がある。これについて、[[飛鳥浄御原令]]期には明法博士が置かれていたが大宝令では設置されなかったとする説と大宝令編纂に関わった「令官」のこととする説、明法を原義のように“法律に明るい”と解して単に「法律に通じた学者」とする説がある。（久木幸男『日本古代学校の研究』（[[1990年]]、[[玉川大学]]出版部）ISBN 4-4720-7981-X）</ref>。当初の名称は'''律学博士'''（りつがくはかせ）であったが、[[天平]]2年[[3月27日 (旧暦)|3月27日]]（[[730年]][[4月18日]]）に'''[[明法道|明法生]]'''が設置されてから遠くない時期に'''明法博士'''と改称されたとされている。明法博士の下には'''明法得業生'''（みょうほうとくぎょうしょう）2名と'''明法生'''（みょうほうしょう）10名（後に20名）があった。後には、[[陣定]]などの朝議に際して法律的な見解を記した[[明法勘文]]を作成・提出することも重要な職務となった。[[平安時代]]中期には[[讃岐氏]]や[[惟宗氏]]の世襲の傾向が見られたが、中世以降には両氏に代わって、名望の[[坂上氏]]及び[[中原氏]]の世襲となった<ref>[[建久]]2年（[[1191年]]）4月1日に[[鎌倉幕府]][[政所|政所別当]]・[[大江広元|中原広元]]が[[源通親|土御門通親]]の推挙により明法博士となった。この人事について[[九条兼実]]は[[明経道]]系の中原氏出身（後に[[大江氏]]に改姓）である広元の就任は先例に反すると激しく非難している（『[[玉葉]]』4月1日条）。もっとも広元の在任期間は短く、同年11月5日に辞任している（『[[吾妻鏡]]』建久3年3月2日条）。この辞任については、自由任官による[[源頼朝]]の不快が原因とする解釈が一般的であるが、頼朝が在京武力掌握のために検非違使庁を幕府の管理下に置く構想を抱き、検非違使庁の法曹部門を担当する明法博士に広元を就任させたものの、[[建久二年の強訴]]を防げなかった責任を取って辞任したのではないかとする見解もある（佐伯智広「一条能保と鎌倉初期公武関係」『古代文化』564、2006年）。</ref>。

revision.text:
'''明法博士'''（みょうほうはかせ）は、古代日本の[[律令制]]下において[[大学寮]]に属した[[官職]]の一つ。[[令外官]]。定員2名で、当初は正七位下相当。 後に名門出身者で占められるようになった。
[[神亀]]5年[[7月21日 (旧暦)|7月21日]]（[[728年]][[8月30日]]）の[[格式|格]]において[[文章博士]]とともに設置された<ref>『[[続日本紀]]』[[大宝_(日本)|大宝]]元年8月戊申（8日）条に[[西海道]]を除く各道に明法博士を派遣して[[大宝律令|大宝令]]について説明させたとする記事がある。これについて、[[飛鳥浄御原令]]期には明法博士が置かれていたが大宝令では設置されなかったとする説と大宝令編纂に関わった「令官」のこととする説、明法を原義のように“法律に明るい”と解して単に「法律に通じた学者」とする説がある。（久木幸男『日本古代学校の研究』（[[1990年]]、[[玉川大学]]出版部）ISBN 4-4720-7981-X）</ref>。当初の名称は'''律学博士'''（りつがくはかせ）であったが、[[天平]]2年[[3月27日 (旧暦)|3月27日]]（[[730年]][[4月18日]]）に'''[[明法道|明法生]]'''が設置されてから遠くない時期に'''明法博士'''と改称されたとされている。明法博士の下には'''明法得業生'''（みょうほうとくぎょうしょう）2名と'''明法生'''（みょうほうしょう）10名（後に20名）があった。後には、[[陣定]]などの朝議に際して法律的な見解を記した[[明法勘文]]を作成・提出することも重要な職務となった。[[平安時代]]中期には[[讃岐氏]]や[[惟宗氏]]の世襲の傾向が見られたが、中世以降には両氏に代わって、名望の[[坂上氏]]及び[[中原氏]]の世襲となった<ref>[[建久]]2年（[[1191年]]）4月1日に[[鎌倉幕府]][[政所|政所別当]]・[[大江広元|中原広元]]が[[源通親|土御門通親]]の推挙により明法博士となった。この人事について[[九条兼実]]は[[明経道]]系の中原氏出身（後に[[大江氏]]に改姓）である広元の就任は先例に反すると激しく非難している（『[[玉葉]]』4月1日条）。もっとも広元の在任期間は短く、同年11月5日に辞任している（『[[吾妻鏡]]』建久3年3月2日条）。この辞任については、自由任官による[[源頼朝]]の不快が原因とする解釈が一般的であるが、頼朝が在京武力掌握のために検非違使庁を幕府の管理下に置く構想を抱き、検非違使庁の法曹部門を担当する明法博士に広元を就任させたものの、[[建久二年の強訴]]を防げなかった責任を取って辞任したのではないかとする見解もある（佐伯智広「一条能保と鎌倉初期公武関係」『古代文化』564、2006年）。</ref>。
== 脚注 == <references />
== 関連項目 == * [[大学寮]] * [[明法道]] * [[勢多章甫]] - 最後の明法博士 * [[律令制]] * [[律令法]] * [[律令]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:みようほうはかせ}} [[Category:日本の律令制]] [[Category:令外官]] [[Category:大学寮]] [[Category:日本の法学史]]

[[ja:明法博士]] [[sn:Myōhō Hakase]] [[sn:Myōhō Hakase]]

[[ja:明法博士]] [[sn:Myōhō Hakase]] [[sn:Myōhō Hakase]] {{Infobox officeholder | name = '''Myōhō Hakase''' (明法博士) | native_name = 明法博士 | title = 明法博士 | department = [[Daigakuryō]] | type = {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}} official | established = [[Shōmu]] Era, 728 CE | abolished = Medieval period | related_posts = [[Meihō-dō]] official, [[Ritsuryō]] scholar }}
'''Myōhō Hakase''' (明法博士, みょうほうはかせ) was an [[official]] belonging to the {{ill|Daigakuryō|ja|大学寮}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}} in ancient Japan. It was an {{ill|Extraordinary Office|ja|令外官}} with two positions. Initially, it was ranked as ''Shōshichii-ge'' (正七位下). Later, it came to be filled by members of prestigious families.
== History == Myōhō Hakase was established along with {{ill|Monjō Hakase|ja|文章博士}} under the {{ill|Kaku|ja|格式}} issued on {{ill|July 21 (lunar calendar)|ja|7月21日 (旧暦)}} of {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀}} 5 (August 30, 728)<ref> {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, entry for Daibō (8th day) of the 8th month of Taika Gannen. This record mentions dispatching Myōhō Hakase to various circuits (excluding Saikaidō) to explain the {{ill|Taihō Ritsuryō|ja|大宝律令}}. There are theories that Myōhō Hakase existed during the {{ill|Asuka Kiyomihara Ritsuryō|ja|飛鳥浄御原令}} period but was not established under the Taihō Ritsuryō, and that the term referred to 'officials knowledgeable in laws.' (Yukio Hisaki, ''Study of Ancient Japanese Schools'' (1990, Tamagawa University Press) ISBN 4-4720-7981-X).</ref>.
Initially, it was called '''Ritsugaku Hakase''' (律学博士), but after the establishment of {{ill|Meihōsei|ja|明法生}} on {{ill|March 27 (lunar calendar)|ja|3月27日 (旧暦)}} of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 2 (April 18, 730), it was soon renamed '''Myōhō Hakase'''. Under Myōhō Hakase were '''Myōhō Tokugyōshō''' (明法得業生) with 2 positions and '''Myōhōshō''' (明法生) with 10 positions (later increased to 20). Over time, one of the key duties was drafting and submitting {{ill|Meihō Kanbun|ja|明法勘文}}, legal opinions during court discussions like {{ill|Jinden|ja|陣定}}.
By the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the positions became hereditary among the {{ill|Sanuki clan|ja|讃岐氏}} and {{ill|Imomune clan|ja|惟宗氏}}, but during the medieval period, these were replaced by the renowned {{ill|Sakanoue clan|ja|坂上氏}} and {{ill|Nakahara clan|ja|中原氏}}<ref>In {{ill|Kenkū 2|ja|建久}} (1191), {{ill|Nakahara no Hiromoto|ja|大江広元}} was appointed as Myōhō Hakase through the recommendation of {{ill|Tsuchimikado no Michichika|ja|土御門通親}} while serving as the {{ill|Mandokoro Betto|ja|政所別当}} of the {{ill|Kamakura Shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府}}. This appointment was harshly criticized by {{ill|Kujō Kanezane|ja|九条兼実}} as violating precedent, as Hiromoto was of the {{ill|Monjōdō|ja|明経道}} branch of the Nakahara family (who later adopted the Ōe surname). However, Hiromoto resigned on November 5 of that year. It is generally believed that this resignation was due to displeasure from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} over free appointments, but another view suggests that Yoritomo had plans to place the {{ill|Kebiishi|ja|検非違使庁}} under the control of the Shogunate and appointed Hiromoto to oversee the legal aspects but made him resign after the {{ill|Kenkū 2 Uprising|ja|建久二年の強訴}} could not be prevented (Tomohiro Saeki, ''Ichijō Yoshiyasu and Early Kamakura Public-Private Relations,'' ''Ancient Culture'' 564, 2006).</ref>.
== Notes == <references />
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Daigakuryō|ja|大学寮}} * {{ill|Meihō-dō|ja|明法道}} * {{ill|Seta Shōho|ja|勢多章甫}} – The last Myōhō Hakase * {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}} * {{ill|Ritsuryō law|ja|律令法}} * {{ill|Ritsuryō|ja|律令}}
{{DEFAULTSORT:みようほうはかせ}} [[Category:Ritsuryō system of Japan]] [[Category:Extraordinary Offices]] [[Category:Daigakuryō]] [[Category:Legal history of Japan]] [[ja:明法博士]]

[[ja:明法博士]] [[sn:Myōhō Hakase]] [[sn:Myōhō Hakase]] {{Infobox officeholder | name = '''Myōhō Hakase''' (明法博士) | native_name = 明法博士 | title = 明法博士 | department = [[Daigakuryō]] | type = {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}} official | established = [[Shōmu]] Era, 728 CE | abolished = Medieval period | related_posts = [[Meihō-dō]] official, [[Ritsuryō]] scholar }}
'''Myōhō Hakase''' (明法博士, みょうほうはかせ) was an [[official]] belonging to the {{ill|Daigakuryō|ja|大学寮}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}} in ancient Japan. It was an {{ill|Extraordinary Office|ja|令外官}} with two positions. Initially, it was ranked as ''Shōshichii-ge'' (正七位下). Later, it came to be filled by members of prestigious families.
== History == Myōhō Hakase was established along with {{ill|Monjō Hakase|ja|文章博士}} under the {{ill|Kaku|ja|格式}} issued on {{ill|July 21 (lunar calendar)|ja|7月21日 (旧暦)}} of {{ill|Shinki|ja|神亀}} 5 (August 30, 728)<ref> {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|ja|続日本紀}}, entry for Daibō (8th day) of the 8th month of Taika Gannen. This record mentions dispatching Myōhō Hakase to various circuits (excluding Saikaidō) to explain the {{ill|Taihō Ritsuryō|ja|大宝律令}}. There are theories that Myōhō Hakase existed during the {{ill|Asuka Kiyomihara Ritsuryō|ja|飛鳥浄御原令}} period but was not established under the Taihō Ritsuryō, and that the term referred to 'officials knowledgeable in laws.' (Yukio Hisaki, ''Study of Ancient Japanese Schools'' (1990, Tamagawa University Press) ISBN 4-4720-7981-X).</ref>.
Initially, it was called '''Ritsugaku Hakase''' (律学博士), but after the establishment of {{ill|Meihōsei|ja|明法生}} on {{ill|March 27 (lunar calendar)|ja|3月27日 (旧暦)}} of {{ill|Tenpyō|ja|天平}} 2 (April 18, 730), it was soon renamed '''Myōhō Hakase'''. Under Myōhō Hakase were '''Myōhō Tokugyōshō''' (明法得業生) with 2 positions and '''Myōhōshō''' (明法生) with 10 positions (later increased to 20). Over time, one of the key duties was drafting and submitting {{ill|Meihō Kanbun|ja|明法勘文}}, legal opinions during court discussions like {{ill|Jinden|ja|陣定}}.
By the mid-{{ill|Heian period|ja|平安時代}}, the positions became hereditary among the {{ill|Sanuki clan|ja|讃岐氏}} and {{ill|Imomune clan|ja|惟宗氏}}, but during the medieval period, these were replaced by the renowned {{ill|Sakanoue clan|ja|坂上氏}} and {{ill|Nakahara clan|ja|中原氏}}<ref>In {{ill|Kenkū 2|ja|建久}} (1191), {{ill|Nakahara no Hiromoto|ja|大江広元}} was appointed as Myōhō Hakase through the recommendation of {{ill|Tsuchimikado no Michichika|ja|土御門通親}} while serving as the {{ill|Mandokoro Betto|ja|政所別当}} of the {{ill|Kamakura Shogunate|ja|鎌倉幕府}}. This appointment was harshly criticized by {{ill|Kujō Kanezane|ja|九条兼実}} as violating precedent, as Hiromoto was of the {{ill|Monjōdō|ja|明経道}} branch of the Nakahara family (who later adopted the Ōe surname). However, Hiromoto resigned on November 5 of that year. It is generally believed that this resignation was due to displeasure from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}} over free appointments, but another view suggests that Yoritomo had plans to place the {{ill|Kebiishi|ja|検非違使庁}} under the control of the Shogunate and appointed Hiromoto to oversee the legal aspects but made him resign after the {{ill|Kenkū 2 Uprising|ja|建久二年の強訴}} could not be prevented (Tomohiro Saeki, ''Ichijō Yoshiyasu and Early Kamakura Public-Private Relations,'' ''Ancient Culture'' 564, 2006).</ref>.
== Notes == <references />
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Daigakuryō|ja|大学寮}} * {{ill|Meihō-dō|ja|明法道}} * {{ill|Seta Shōho|ja|勢多章甫}} – The last Myōhō Hakase * {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}} * {{ill|Ritsuryō law|ja|律令法}} * {{ill|Ritsuryō|ja|律令}}

revision.text:
'''明法博士'''（みょうほうはかせ）は、古代日本の[[律令制]]下において[[大学寮]]に属した[[官職]]の一つ。[[令外官]]。定員2名で、当初は正七位下相当。 後に名門出身者で占められるようになった。
[[神亀]]5年[[7月21日 (旧暦)|7月21日]]（[[728年]][[8月30日]]）の[[格式|格]]において[[文章博士]]とともに設置された<ref>『[[続日本紀]]』[[大宝_(日本)|大宝]]元年8月戊申（8日）条に[[西海道]]を除く各道に明法博士を派遣して[[大宝律令|大宝令]]について説明させたとする記事がある。これについて、[[飛鳥浄御原令]]期には明法博士が置かれていたが大宝令では設置されなかったとする説と大宝令編纂に関わった「令官」のこととする説、明法を原義のように“法律に明るい”と解して単に「法律に通じた学者」とする説がある。（久木幸男『日本古代学校の研究』（[[1990年]]、[[玉川大学]]出版部）ISBN 4-4720-7981-X）</ref>。当初の名称は'''律学博士'''（りつがくはかせ）であったが、[[天平]]2年[[3月27日 (旧暦)|3月27日]]（[[730年]][[4月18日]]）に'''[[明法道|明法生]]'''が設置されてから遠くない時期に'''明法博士'''と改称されたとされている。明法博士の下には'''明法得業生'''（みょうほうとくぎょうしょう）2名と'''明法生'''（みょうほうしょう）10名（後に20名）があった。後には、[[陣定]]などの朝議に際して法律的な見解を記した[[明法勘文]]を作成・提出することも重要な職務となった。[[平安時代]]中期には[[讃岐氏]]や[[惟宗氏]]の世襲の傾向が見られたが、中世以降には両氏に代わって、名望の[[坂上氏]]及び[[中原氏]]の世襲となった<ref>[[建久]]2年（[[1191年]]）4月1日に[[鎌倉幕府]][[政所|政所別当]]・[[大江広元|中原広元]]が[[源通親|土御門通親]]の推挙により明法博士となった。この人事について[[九条兼実]]は[[明経道]]系の中原氏出身（後に[[大江氏]]に改姓）である広元の就任は先例に反すると激しく非難している（『[[玉葉]]』4月1日条）。もっとも広元の在任期間は短く、同年11月5日に辞任している（『[[吾妻鏡]]』建久3年3月2日条）。この辞任については、自由任官による[[源頼朝]]の不快が原因とする解釈が一般的であるが、頼朝が在京武力掌握のために検非違使庁を幕府の管理下に置く構想を抱き、検非違使庁の法曹部門を担当する明法博士に広元を就任させたものの、[[建久二年の強訴]]を防げなかった責任を取って辞任したのではないかとする見解もある（佐伯智広「一条能保と鎌倉初期公武関係」『古代文化』564、2006年）。</ref>。
== 脚注 == <references />
== 関連項目 == * [[大学寮]] * [[明法道]] * [[勢多章甫]] - 最後の明法博士 * [[律令制]] * [[律令法]] * [[律令]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:みようほうはかせ}} [[Category:日本の律令制]] [[Category:令外官]] [[Category:大学寮]] [[Category:日本の法学史]]

[[神亀]]5年[[7月21日 (旧暦)|7月21日]]（[[728年]][[8月30日]]）の[[格式|格]]において[[文章博士]]とともに設置された<ref>『[[続日本紀]]』[[大宝_(日本)|大宝]]元年8月戊申（8日）条に[[西海道]]を除く各道に明法博士を派遣して[[大宝律令|大宝令]]について説明させたとする記事がある。これについて、[[飛鳥浄御原令]]期には明法博士が置かれていたが大宝令では設置されなかったとする説と大宝令編纂に関わった「令官」のこととする説、明法を原義のように“法律に明るい”と解して単に「法律に通じた学者」とする説がある。（久木幸男『日本古代学校の研究』（[[1990年]]、[[玉川大学]]出版部）ISBN 4-4720-7981-X）</ref>。当初の名称は'''律学博士'''（りつがくはかせ）であったが、[[天平]]2年[[3月27日 (旧暦)|3月27日]]（[[730年]][[4月18日]]）に'''[[明法道|明法生]]'''が設置されてから遠くない時期に'''明法博士'''と改称されたとされている。明法博士の下には'''明法得業生'''（みょうほうとくぎょうしょう）2名と'''明法生'''（みょうほうしょう）10名（後に20名）があった。後には、[[陣定]]などの朝議に際して法律的な見解を記した[[明法勘文]]を作成・提出することも重要な職務となった。[[平安時代]]中期には[[讃岐氏]]や[[惟宗氏]]の世襲の傾向が見られたが、中世以降には両氏に代わって、名望の[[坂上氏]]及び[[中原氏]]の世襲となった<ref>[[建久]]2年（[[1191年]]）4月1日に[[鎌倉幕府]][[政所|政所別当]]・[[大江広元|中原広元]]が[[源通親|土御門通親]]の推挙により明法博士となった。この人事について[[九条兼実]]は[[明経道]]系の中原氏出身（後に[[大江氏]]に改姓）である広元の就任は先例に反すると激しく非難している（『[[玉葉]]』4月1日条）。もっとも広元の在任期間は短く、同年11月5日に辞任している（『[[吾妻鏡]]』建久3年3月2日条）。この辞任については、自由任官による[[源頼朝]]の不快が原因とする解釈が一般的であるが、頼朝が在京武力掌握のために検非違使庁を幕府の管理下に置く構想を抱き、検非違使庁の法曹部門を担当する明法博士に広元を就任させたものの、[[建久二年の強訴]]を防げなかった責任を取って辞任したのではないかとする見解もある（佐伯智広「一条能保と鎌倉初期公武関係」『古代文化』564、2006年）。</ref>。

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:明法博士]][[zh:明法博士]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11512424]] Wikidata name: myōbō hakase [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox officeholder | name = '''Myōhō Hakase''' (明法博士) | native_name = 明法博士 | title = 明法博士 | department = [[Daigakuryō]] | type = [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] official | established = [[Shōmu]] Era, 728 CE | abolished = Medieval period | related_posts = [[Meihō-dō]] official, [[Ritsuryō]] scholar }}
'''Myōhō Hakase''' (明法博士, みょうほうはかせ) was an [[:en:Official|Official]] belonging to the [[:en:Daigaku-ryō|Daigaku-ryō]] under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] in ancient Japan. It was an {{ill|Extraordinary Office|ja|令外官|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Extraordinary Office|qq=|1=East Asian extra-statutory office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378315}} with two positions. Initially, it was Ranked as ''Shōshichii-ge'' (正七位下). Later, it came to be filled by members of prestigious families.
== History == Myōhō Hakase was established along with {{ill|Monjō Hakase|ja|文章博士|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monjō Hakase|qq=|1=monjō hakase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500074}} under the {{ill|Kaku|ja|格式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaku|qq=|1=ge and shi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11536498}} issued on {{ill|July 21 (lunar calendar)|ja|7月21日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 21 (lunar calendar)|qq=|1=21st day of the 7th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q839764}} of [[:en:Jinki (era)|Shinki]] 5 (August 30, 728)<ref> [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], entry for Daibō (8th day) of the 8th month of Taika Gannen. This record mentions dispatching Myōhō Hakase to various circuits (excluding Saikaidō) to explain the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Ritsuryō]]. There are theories that Myōhō Hakase existed during the [[:en:Asuka Kiyomihara Code|Asuka Kiyomihara Ritsuryō]] period but was not established under the Taihō Ritsuryō, and that the term referred to 'officials knowledgeable in laws.' (Yukio Hisaki, ''Study of Ancient Japanese Schools'' (1990, Tamagawa University Press) ISBN 4-4720-7981-X).</ref>.
Initially, it was called '''Ritsugaku Hakase''' (律学博士), but after the establishment of {{ill|Meihōsei|ja|明法生|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meihōsei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明法道|1=Myōbōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512427}} on {{ill|March 27 (lunar calendar)|ja|3月27日 (旧暦)|1=March 27 (Lunisolar)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 27 (lunar calendar)|qq=}} of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 2 (April 18, 730), it was soon renamed '''Myōhō Hakase'''. Under Myōhō Hakase were '''Myōhō Tokugyōshō''' (明法得業生) with 2 positions and '''Myōhōshō''' (明法生) with 10 positions (later increased to 20). Over time, one of the key duties was drafting and submitting {{ill|Meihō Kanbun|ja|明法勘文|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meihō Kanbun|qq=|1=myōbō kanmon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512423}}, legal opinions during court discussions like {{ill|Jinden|ja|陣定|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinden|qq=|1=jinnosadame|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658177}}.
By the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the positions became hereditary among the {{ill|Sanuki clan|ja|讃岐氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki clan|qq=|1=Sanuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929774}} and {{ill|Imomune clan|ja|惟宗氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imomune clan|qq=|1=Koremune clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492955}}, but during the medieval period, these were replaced by the renowned {{ill|Sakanoue clan|ja|坂上氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue clan|qq=|1=Sakanoue clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10928477}} and {{ill|Nakahara clan|ja|中原氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahara clan|qq=|1=Nakahara clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11363164}}<ref>In [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkū 2]] (1191), [[:en:Ōe no Hiromoto|Nakahara no Hiromoto]] was appointed as Myōhō Hakase through the recommendation of {{ill|Tsuchimikado no Michichika|ja|土御門通親|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado no Michichika|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源通親|1=Minamoto no Michichika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564317}} while serving as the {{ill|Mandokoro Betto|ja|政所別当|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mandokoro Betto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura Shogunate]]. This appointment was harshly criticized by [[:en:Fujiwara no Kanezane|Kujō Kanezane]] as violating precedent, as Hiromoto was of the {{ill|Monjōdō|ja|明経道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monjōdō|qq=|1=myōgyōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11241198}} branch of the Nakahara family (who later adopted the Ōe surname). However, Hiromoto resigned on November 5 of that year. It is generally believed that this resignation was due to displeasure from [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] over free appointments, but another view suggests that Yoritomo had plans to place the {{ill|Office of Police and Justice|lt=Office of Police and Justice|lt=Kebiishi|ja|検非違使庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kebiishi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 検非違使}} under the control of the Shogunate and appointed Hiromoto to oversee the legal aspects but made him resign after the {{ill|Kenkū 2 Uprising|ja|建久二年の強訴|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkū 2 Uprising|qq=|1=Gōso of 1191|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486617}} could not be prevented (Tomohiro Saeki, ''Ichijō Yoshiyasu and Early Kamakura Public-Private Relations,'' ''Ancient Culture'' 564, 2006).</ref>.
== Notes == <references />

[[ja:明法博士]] [[ja:%E6%98%8E%E6%B3%95%E5%8D%9A%E5%A3%AB]] [[Category:Extraordinary Government Officials of Ancient Japan]] [[Category:University Dormitories]] [[Category:Ritsuryō System of Japan]] [[Category:Historical jurisprudence in Japan]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:明法博士]] {{wikidata link|Q11512424}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:明法博士]][[zh:明法博士]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11512424]] Wikidata name: myōbō hakase [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox officeholder | name = '''Myōhō Hakase''' (明法博士) | native_name = 明法博士 | title = 明法博士 | department = [[Daigakuryō]] | type = [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] official | established = [[Shōmu]] Era, 728 CE | abolished = Medieval period | related_posts = [[Meihō-dō]] official, [[Ritsuryō]] scholar }}
'''Myōhō Hakase''' (明法博士, みょうほうはかせ) was an [[:en:Official|Official]] belonging to the [[:en:Daigaku-ryō|Daigaku-ryō]] under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] in ancient Japan. It was an {{ill|Extraordinary Office|ja|令外官|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Extraordinary Office|qq=|1=East Asian extra-statutory office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378315}} with two positions. Initially, it was Ranked as ''Shōshichii-ge'' (正七位下). Later, it came to be filled by members of prestigious families.
== History == Myōhō Hakase was established along with {{ill|Monjō Hakase|ja|文章博士|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monjō Hakase|qq=|1=monjō hakase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500074}} under the {{ill|Kaku|ja|格式|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaku|qq=|1=ge and shi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11536498}} issued on {{ill|July 21 (lunar calendar)|ja|7月21日 (旧暦)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 21 (lunar calendar)|qq=|1=21st day of the 7th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q839764}} of [[:en:Jinki (era)|Shinki]] 5 (August 30, 728)<ref> [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], entry for Daibō (8th day) of the 8th month of Taika Gannen. This record mentions dispatching Myōhō Hakase to various circuits (excluding Saikaidō) to explain the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Ritsuryō]]. There are theories that Myōhō Hakase existed during the [[:en:Asuka Kiyomihara Code|Asuka Kiyomihara Ritsuryō]] period but was not established under the Taihō Ritsuryō, and that the term referred to 'officials knowledgeable in laws.' (Yukio Hisaki, ''Study of Ancient Japanese Schools'' (1990, Tamagawa University Press) ISBN 4-4720-7981-X).</ref>.
Initially, it was called '''Ritsugaku Hakase''' (律学博士), but after the establishment of {{ill|Meihōsei|ja|明法生|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meihōsei|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明法道|1=Myōbōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512427}} on {{ill|March 27 (lunar calendar)|ja|3月27日 (旧暦)|1=March 27 (Lunisolar)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 27 (lunar calendar)|qq=}} of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 2 (April 18, 730), it was soon renamed '''Myōhō Hakase'''. Under Myōhō Hakase were '''Myōhō Tokugyōshō''' (明法得業生) with 2 positions and '''Myōhōshō''' (明法生) with 10 positions (later increased to 20). Over time, one of the key duties was drafting and submitting {{ill|Meihō Kanbun|ja|明法勘文|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meihō Kanbun|qq=|1=myōbō kanmon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512423}}, legal opinions during court discussions like {{ill|Jinden|ja|陣定|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinden|qq=|1=jinnosadame|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658177}}.
By the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the positions became hereditary among the {{ill|Sanuki clan|ja|讃岐氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki clan|qq=|1=Sanuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929774}} and {{ill|Imomune clan|ja|惟宗氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imomune clan|qq=|1=Koremune clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492955}}, but during the medieval period, these were replaced by the renowned {{ill|Sakanoue clan|ja|坂上氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue clan|qq=|1=Sakanoue clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10928477}} and {{ill|Nakahara clan|ja|中原氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahara clan|qq=|1=Nakahara clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11363164}}<ref>In [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkū 2]] (1191), [[:en:Ōe no Hiromoto|Nakahara no Hiromoto]] was appointed as Myōhō Hakase through the recommendation of {{ill|Tsuchimikado no Michichika|ja|土御門通親|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado no Michichika|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源通親|1=Minamoto no Michichika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564317}} while serving as the {{ill|Mandokoro Betto|ja|政所別当|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mandokoro Betto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura Shogunate]]. This appointment was harshly criticized by [[:en:Fujiwara no Kanezane|Kujō Kanezane]] as violating precedent, as Hiromoto was of the {{ill|Monjōdō|ja|明経道|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monjōdō|qq=|1=myōgyōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11241198}} branch of the Nakahara family (who later adopted the Ōe surname). However, Hiromoto resigned on November 5 of that year. It is generally believed that this resignation was due to displeasure from [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] over free appointments, but another view suggests that Yoritomo had plans to place the {{ill|Office of Police and Justice|lt=Office of Police and Justice|lt=Kebiishi|ja|検非違使庁|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kebiishi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 検非違使}} under the control of the Shogunate and appointed Hiromoto to oversee the legal aspects but made him resign after the {{ill|Kenkū 2 Uprising|ja|建久二年の強訴|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkū 2 Uprising|qq=|1=Gōso of 1191|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486617}} could not be prevented (Tomohiro Saeki, ''Ichijō Yoshiyasu and Early Kamakura Public-Private Relations,'' ''Ancient Culture'' 564, 2006).</ref>.

It is generally believed that this resignation was due to displeasure from [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] over free appointments, but another view suggests that Yoritomo had plans to place the {{ill|Office of Police and Justice|lt=Office of Police and Justice|lt=Kebiishi|ja|検非違使庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kebiishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 検非違使}} under the control of the Shogunate and appointed Hiromoto to oversee the legal aspects but made him resign after the {{ill|Kenkū 2 Uprising|ja|建久二年の強訴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkū 2 Uprising|1=Gōso of 1191|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486617}} could not be prevented (Tomohiro Saeki, ''Ichijō Yoshiyasu and Early Kamakura Public-Private Relations,'' ''Ancient Culture'' 564, 2006).</ref>.
== Notes == <references />

[[ja:明法博士]] [[ja:%E6%98%8E%E6%B3%95%E5%8D%9A%E5%A3%AB]] [[Category:Extraordinary Government Officials of Ancient Japan]] [[Category:University Dormitories]] [[Category:Ritsuryō System of Japan]] [[Category:Historical jurisprudence in Japan]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:明法博士]] {{wikidata link|Q11512424}}

revision.comment: Bot: Add Wikidata no short description - based on Wikidata English short description
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:明法博士]][[zh:明法博士]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11512424]] Wikidata name: myōbō hakase [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox officeholder | name = '''Myōhō Hakase''' (明法博士) | native_name = 明法博士 | title = 明法博士 | department = [[Daigakuryō]] | type = [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] official | established = [[Shōmu]] Era, 728 CE | abolished = Medieval period | related_posts = [[Meihō-dō]] official, [[Ritsuryō]] scholar }}
'''Myōhō Hakase''' (明法博士, みょうほうはかせ) was an [[:en:Official|Official]] belonging to the [[:en:Daigaku-ryō|Daigaku-ryō]] under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] in ancient Japan. It was an {{ill|Extraordinary Office|ja|令外官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Extraordinary Office|1=East Asian extra-statutory office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378315}} with two positions. Initially, it was Ranked as ''Shōshichii-ge'' (正七位下). Later, it came to be filled by members of prestigious families.
== History == Myōhō Hakase was established along with {{ill|Monjō Hakase|ja|文章博士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monjō Hakase|1=monjō hakase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500074}} under the {{ill|Kaku|ja|格式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaku|1=ge and shi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11536498}} issued on {{ill|July 21 (lunar calendar)|ja|7月21日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 21 (lunar calendar)|1=21st day of the 7th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q839764}} of [[:en:Jinki (era)|Shinki]] 5 (August 30, 728)<ref> [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], entry for Daibō (8th day) of the 8th month of Taika Gannen. This record mentions dispatching Myōhō Hakase to various circuits (excluding Saikaidō) to explain the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Ritsuryō]]. There are theories that Myōhō Hakase existed during the [[:en:Asuka Kiyomihara Code|Asuka Kiyomihara Ritsuryō]] period but was not established under the Taihō Ritsuryō, and that the term referred to 'officials knowledgeable in laws.' (Yukio Hisaki, ''Study of Ancient Japanese Schools'' (1990, Tamagawa University Press) ISBN 4-4720-7981-X).</ref>.
Initially, it was called '''Ritsugaku Hakase''' (律学博士), but after the establishment of {{ill|Meihōsei|ja|明法生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meihōsei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明法道|1=Myōbōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512427}} on {{ill|March 27 (lunar calendar)|ja|3月27日 (旧暦)|1=March 27 (Lunisolar)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 27 (lunar calendar)|qq=}} of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 2 (April 18, 730), it was soon renamed '''Myōhō Hakase'''. Under Myōhō Hakase were '''Myōhō Tokugyōshō''' (明法得業生) with 2 positions and '''Myōhōshō''' (明法生) with 10 positions (later increased to 20). Over time, one of the key duties was drafting and submitting {{ill|Meihō Kanbun|ja|明法勘文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meihō Kanbun|1=myōbō kanmon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512423}}, legal opinions during court discussions like {{ill|Jinden|ja|陣定|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinden|1=jinnosadame|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658177}}.
By the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the positions became hereditary among the {{ill|Sanuki clan|ja|讃岐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki clan|1=Sanuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929774}} and {{ill|Imomune clan|ja|惟宗氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imomune clan|1=Koremune clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492955}}, but during the medieval period, these were replaced by the renowned {{ill|Sakanoue clan|ja|坂上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue clan|1=Sakanoue clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10928477}} and {{ill|Nakahara clan|ja|中原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahara clan|1=Nakahara clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11363164}}<ref>In [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkū 2]] (1191), [[:en:Ōe no Hiromoto|Nakahara no Hiromoto]] was appointed as Myōhō Hakase through the recommendation of {{ill|Tsuchimikado no Michichika|ja|土御門通親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado no Michichika|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源通親|1=Minamoto no Michichika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564317}} while serving as the {{ill|Mandokoro Betto|ja|政所別当|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mandokoro Betto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura Shogunate]]. This appointment was harshly criticized by [[:en:Fujiwara no Kanezane|Kujō Kanezane]] as violating precedent, as Hiromoto was of the {{ill|Monjōdō|ja|明経道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monjōdō|1=myōgyōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11241198}} branch of the Nakahara family (who later adopted the Ōe surname). However, Hiromoto resigned on November 5 of that year. It is generally believed that this resignation was due to displeasure from [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] over free appointments, but another view suggests that Yoritomo had plans to place the {{ill|Office of Police and Justice|lt=Office of Police and Justice|lt=Kebiishi|ja|検非違使庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kebiishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 検非違使}} under the control of the Shogunate and appointed Hiromoto to oversee the legal aspects but made him resign after the {{ill|Kenkū 2 Uprising|ja|建久二年の強訴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkū 2 Uprising|1=Gōso of 1191|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486617}} could not be prevented (Tomohiro Saeki, ''Ichijō Yoshiyasu and Early Kamakura Public-Private Relations,'' ''Ancient Culture'' 564, 2006).</ref>.
== Notes == <references />
== Related Topics == * [[:en:Daigaku-ryō|Daigaku-ryō]] * {{ill|Meihō-dō|ja|明法道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meihō-dō|1=Myōbōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512427}} * {{ill|Seta Shōho|ja|勢多章甫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seta Shōho|1=Seta Norimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q84977839}} – The last Myōhō Hakase * [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] * {{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Ritsuryō law|ja|律令法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō law|qq=}} * [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryō]]

It is generally believed that this resignation was due to displeasure from [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] over free appointments, but another view suggests that Yoritomo had plans to place the {{ill|Office of Police and Justice|lt=Office of Police and Justice|lt=Kebiishi|ja|検非違使庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kebiishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 検非違使}} under the control of the Shogunate and appointed Hiromoto to oversee the legal aspects but made him resign after the {{ill|Kenkū 2 Uprising|ja|建久二年の強訴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkū 2 Uprising|1=Gōso of 1191|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486617}} could not be prevented (Tomohiro Saeki, ''Ichijō Yoshiyasu and Early Kamakura Public-Private Relations,'' ''Ancient Culture'' 564, 2006).</ref>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:明法博士]][[zh:明法博士]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11512424]] Wikidata name: myōbō hakase [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox officeholder | name = '''Myōhō Hakase''' (明法博士) | native_name = 明法博士 | title = 明法博士 | department = [[Daigakuryō]] | type = [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] official | established = [[Shōmu]] Era, 728 CE | abolished = Medieval period | related_posts = [[Meihō-dō]] official, [[Ritsuryō]] scholar }}
'''Myōhō Hakase''' (明法博士, みょうほうはかせ) was an [[:en:Official|Official]] belonging to the [[:en:Daigaku-ryō|Daigaku-ryō]] under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] in ancient Japan. It was an {{ill|Extraordinary Office|ja|令外官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Extraordinary Office|1=East Asian extra-statutory office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378315}} with two positions. Initially, it was Ranked as ''Shōshichii-ge'' (正七位下). Later, it came to be filled by members of prestigious families.
== History == Myōhō Hakase was established along with {{ill|Monjō Hakase|ja|文章博士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monjō Hakase|1=monjō hakase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500074}} under the {{ill|Kaku|ja|格式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaku|1=ge and shi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11536498}} issued on {{ill|July 21 (lunar calendar)|ja|7月21日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 21 (lunar calendar)|1=21st day of the 7th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q839764}} of [[:en:Jinki (era)|Shinki]] 5 (August 30, 728)<ref> [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], entry for Daibō (8th day) of the 8th month of Taika Gannen. This record mentions dispatching Myōhō Hakase to various circuits (excluding Saikaidō) to explain the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Ritsuryō]]. There are theories that Myōhō Hakase existed during the [[:en:Asuka Kiyomihara Code|Asuka Kiyomihara Ritsuryō]] period but was not established under the Taihō Ritsuryō, and that the term referred to 'officials knowledgeable in laws.' (Yukio Hisaki, ''Study of Ancient Japanese Schools'' (1990, Tamagawa University Press) ISBN 4-4720-7981-X).</ref>.
Initially, it was called '''Ritsugaku Hakase''' (律学博士), but after the establishment of {{ill|Meihōsei|ja|明法生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meihōsei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明法道|1=Myōbōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512427}} on {{ill|March 27 (lunar calendar)|ja|3月27日 (旧暦)|1=March 27 (Lunisolar)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 27 (lunar calendar)|qq=}} of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 2 (April 18, 730), it was soon renamed '''Myōhō Hakase'''. Under Myōhō Hakase were '''Myōhō Tokugyōshō''' (明法得業生) with 2 positions and '''Myōhōshō''' (明法生) with 10 positions (later increased to 20). Over time, one of the key duties was drafting and submitting {{ill|Meihō Kanbun|ja|明法勘文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meihō Kanbun|1=myōbō kanmon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512423}}, legal opinions during court discussions like {{ill|Jinden|ja|陣定|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinden|1=jinnosadame|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658177}}.
By the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the positions became hereditary among the {{ill|Sanuki clan|ja|讃岐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki clan|1=Sanuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929774}} and {{ill|Imomune clan|ja|惟宗氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imomune clan|1=Koremune clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492955}}, but during the medieval period, these were replaced by the renowned {{ill|Sakanoue clan|ja|坂上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue clan|1=Sakanoue clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10928477}} and {{ill|Nakahara clan|ja|中原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahara clan|1=Nakahara clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11363164}}<ref>In [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkū 2]] (1191), [[:en:Ōe no Hiromoto|Nakahara no Hiromoto]] was appointed as Myōhō Hakase through the recommendation of {{ill|Tsuchimikado no Michichika|ja|土御門通親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado no Michichika|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源通親|1=Minamoto no Michichika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564317}} while serving as the {{ill|Mandokoro Betto|ja|政所別当|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mandokoro Betto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura Shogunate]]. This appointment was harshly criticized by [[:en:Fujiwara no Kanezane|Kujō Kanezane]] as violating precedent, as Hiromoto was of the {{ill|Monjōdō|ja|明経道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monjōdō|1=myōgyōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11241198}} branch of the Nakahara family (who later adopted the Ōe surname). However, Hiromoto resigned on November 5 of that year. It is generally believed that this resignation was due to displeasure from [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] over free appointments, but another view suggests that Yoritomo had plans to place the {{ill|Office of Police and Justice|lt=Office of Police and Justice|lt=Kebiishi|ja|検非違使庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kebiishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 検非違使}} under the control of the Shogunate and appointed Hiromoto to oversee the legal aspects but made him resign after the {{ill|Kenkū 2 Uprising|ja|建久二年の強訴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkū 2 Uprising|1=Gōso of 1191|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486617}} could not be prevented (Tomohiro Saeki, ''Ichijō Yoshiyasu and Early Kamakura Public-Private Relations,'' ''Ancient Culture'' 564, 2006).</ref>.

== Related Topics == * [[:en:Daigaku-ryō|Daigaku-ryō]] * {{ill|Meihō-dō|ja|明法道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meihō-dō|1=Myōbōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512427}} * {{ill|Seta Shōho|ja|勢多章甫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seta Shōho|1=Seta Norimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q84977839}} – The last Myōhō Hakase * [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] * {{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Ritsuryō law|ja|律令法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō law|qq=}} * [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryō]]

It is generally believed that this resignation was due to displeasure from [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] over free appointments, but another view suggests that Yoritomo had plans to place the {{ill|Office of Police and Justice|lt=Office of Police and Justice|lt=Kebiishi|ja|検非違使庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kebiishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 検非違使}} under the control of the Shogunate and appointed Hiromoto to oversee the legal aspects but made him resign after the {{ill|Kenkū 2 Uprising|ja|建久二年の強訴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkū 2 Uprising|1=Gōso of 1191|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486617}} could not be prevented (Tomohiro Saeki, ''Ichijō Yoshiyasu and Early Kamakura Public-Private Relations,'' ''Ancient Culture'' 564, 2006).</ref>.
== Notes == <references />
== Related Topics == * [[:en:Daigaku-ryō|Daigaku-ryō]] * {{ill|Meihō-dō|ja|明法道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meihō-dō|1=Myōbōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512427}} * {{ill|Seta Shōho|ja|勢多章甫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seta Shōho|1=Seta Norimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q84977839}} – The last Myōhō Hakase * [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] * {{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Ritsuryō law|ja|律令法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō law|qq=}} * [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryō]]

[[ja:明法博士]] [[ja:%E6%98%8E%E6%B3%95%E5%8D%9A%E5%A3%AB]] [[Category:Extraordinary Government Officials of Ancient Japan]] [[Category:University Dormitories]] [[Category:Ritsuryō System of Japan]] [[Category:Historical jurisprudence in Japan]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:明法博士]] {{wikidata link|Q11512424}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:明法博士]][[zh:明法博士]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11512424]] Wikidata name: myōbō hakase [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox officeholder | name = '''Myōhō Hakase''' (明法博士) | native_name = 明法博士 | title = 明法博士 | department = [[Daigakuryō]] | type = [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] official | established = [[Shōmu]] Era, 728 CE | abolished = Medieval period | related_posts = [[Meihō-dō]] official, [[Ritsuryō]] scholar }}
'''Myōhō Hakase''' (明法博士, みょうほうはかせ) was an [[:en:Official|Official]] belonging to the [[:en:Daigaku-ryō|Daigaku-ryō]] under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] in ancient Japan. It was an {{ill|Extraordinary Office|ja|令外官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Extraordinary Office|1=East Asian extra-statutory office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378315}} with two positions. Initially, it was Ranked as ''Shōshichii-ge'' (正七位下). Later, it came to be filled by members of prestigious families.
== History == Myōhō Hakase was established along with {{ill|Monjō Hakase|ja|文章博士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monjō Hakase|1=monjō hakase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500074}} under the {{ill|Kaku|ja|格式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaku|1=ge and shi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11536498}} issued on {{ill|July 21 (lunar calendar)|ja|7月21日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 21 (lunar calendar)|1=21st day of the 7th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q839764}} of [[:en:Jinki (era)|Shinki]] 5 (August 30, 728)<ref> [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], entry for Daibō (8th day) of the 8th month of Taika Gannen. This record mentions dispatching Myōhō Hakase to various circuits (excluding Saikaidō) to explain the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Ritsuryō]]. There are theories that Myōhō Hakase existed during the [[:en:Asuka Kiyomihara Code|Asuka Kiyomihara Ritsuryō]] period but was not established under the Taihō Ritsuryō, and that the term referred to 'officials knowledgeable in laws.' (Yukio Hisaki, ''Study of Ancient Japanese Schools'' (1990, Tamagawa University Press) ISBN 4-4720-7981-X).</ref>.
Initially, it was called '''Ritsugaku Hakase''' (律学博士), but after the establishment of {{ill|Meihōsei|ja|明法生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meihōsei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明法道|1=Myōbōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512427}} on {{ill|March 27 (lunar calendar)|ja|3月27日 (旧暦)|1=March 27 (Lunisolar)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 27 (lunar calendar)|qq=}} of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 2 (April 18, 730), it was soon renamed '''Myōhō Hakase'''. Under Myōhō Hakase were '''Myōhō Tokugyōshō''' (明法得業生) with 2 positions and '''Myōhōshō''' (明法生) with 10 positions (later increased to 20). Over time, one of the key duties was drafting and submitting {{ill|Meihō Kanbun|ja|明法勘文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meihō Kanbun|1=myōbō kanmon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512423}}, legal opinions during court discussions like {{ill|Jinden|ja|陣定|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinden|1=jinnosadame|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658177}}.
By the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the positions became hereditary among the {{ill|Sanuki clan|ja|讃岐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki clan|1=Sanuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929774}} and {{ill|Imomune clan|ja|惟宗氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imomune clan|1=Koremune clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492955}}, but during the medieval period, these were replaced by the renowned {{ill|Sakanoue clan|ja|坂上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue clan|1=Sakanoue clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10928477}} and {{ill|Nakahara clan|ja|中原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahara clan|1=Nakahara clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11363164}}<ref>In [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkū 2]] (1191), [[:en:Ōe no Hiromoto|Nakahara no Hiromoto]] was appointed as Myōhō Hakase through the recommendation of {{ill|Tsuchimikado no Michichika|ja|土御門通親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado no Michichika|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源通親|1=Minamoto no Michichika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564317}} while serving as the {{ill|Mandokoro Betto|ja|政所別当|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mandokoro Betto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura Shogunate]]. This appointment was harshly criticized by [[:en:Fujiwara no Kanezane|Kujō Kanezane]] as violating precedent, as Hiromoto was of the {{ill|Monjōdō|ja|明経道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monjōdō|1=myōgyōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11241198}} branch of the Nakahara family (who later adopted the Ōe surname). However, Hiromoto resigned on November 5 of that year. It is generally believed that this resignation was due to displeasure from [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] over free appointments, but another view suggests that Yoritomo had plans to place the {{ill|Office of Police and Justice|lt=Office of Police and Justice|lt=Kebiishi|ja|検非違使庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kebiishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 検非違使}} under the control of the Shogunate and appointed Hiromoto to oversee the legal aspects but made him resign after the {{ill|Kenkū 2 Uprising|ja|建久二年の強訴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkū 2 Uprising|1=Gōso of 1191|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486617}} could not be prevented (Tomohiro Saeki, ''Ichijō Yoshiyasu and Early Kamakura Public-Private Relations,'' ''Ancient Culture'' 564, 2006).</ref>.
== Notes == <references />
== Related Topics == * [[:en:Daigaku-ryō|Daigaku-ryō]] * {{ill|Meihō-dō|ja|明法道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meihō-dō|1=Myōbōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512427}} * {{ill|Seta Shōho|ja|勢多章甫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seta Shōho|1=Seta Norimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q84977839}} – The last Myōhō Hakase * [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] * {{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Ritsuryō law|ja|律令法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō law|qq=}} * [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryō]]

However, Hiromoto resigned on November 5 of that year. It is generally believed that this resignation was due to displeasure from [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] over free appointments, but another view suggests that Yoritomo had plans to place the {{ill|Office of Police and Justice|lt=Office of Police and Justice|lt=Kebiishi|ja|検非違使庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kebiishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 検非違使}} under the control of the Shogunate and appointed Hiromoto to oversee the legal aspects but made him resign after the {{ill|Kenkū 2 Uprising|ja|建久二年の強訴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkū 2 Uprising|1=Gōso of 1191|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486617}} could not be prevented (Tomohiro Saeki, ''Ichijō Yoshiyasu and Early Kamakura Public-Private Relations,'' ''Ancient Culture'' 564, 2006).</ref>.

[[ja:明法博士]] [[ja:%E6%98%8E%E6%B3%95%E5%8D%9A%E5%A3%AB]] [[Category:Extraordinary Government Officials of Ancient Japan]] [[Category:University Dormitories]] [[Category:Ritsuryō System of Japan]] [[Category:Historical jurisprudence in Japan]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:明法博士]] {{wikidata link|Q11512424}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:明法博士]][[zh:明法博士]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:明法博士]][[zh:明法博士]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:明法博士]][[zh:明法博士]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11512424]] Wikidata name: myōbō hakase [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox officeholder | name = '''Myōhō Hakase''' (明法博士) | native_name = 明法博士 | title = 明法博士 | department = [[Daigakuryō]] | type = [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] official | established = [[Shōmu]] Era, 728 CE | abolished = Medieval period | related_posts = [[Meihō-dō]] official, [[Ritsuryō]] scholar }}
'''Myōhō Hakase''' (明法博士, みょうほうはかせ) was an [[:en:Official|Official]] belonging to the [[:en:Daigaku-ryō|Daigaku-ryō]] under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] in ancient Japan. It was an {{ill|Extraordinary Office|ja|令外官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Extraordinary Office|1=East Asian extra-statutory office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378315}} with two positions. Initially, it was Ranked as ''Shōshichii-ge'' (正七位下). Later, it came to be filled by members of prestigious families.
== History == Myōhō Hakase was established along with {{ill|Monjō Hakase|ja|文章博士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monjō Hakase|1=monjō hakase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500074}} under the {{ill|Kaku|ja|格式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaku|1=ge and shi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11536498}} issued on {{ill|July 21 (lunar calendar)|ja|7月21日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 21 (lunar calendar)|1=21st day of the 7th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q839764}} of [[:en:Jinki (era)|Shinki]] 5 (August 30, 728)<ref> [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], entry for Daibō (8th day) of the 8th month of Taika Gannen. This record mentions dispatching Myōhō Hakase to various circuits (excluding Saikaidō) to explain the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Ritsuryō]]. There are theories that Myōhō Hakase existed during the [[:en:Asuka Kiyomihara Code|Asuka Kiyomihara Ritsuryō]] period but was not established under the Taihō Ritsuryō, and that the term referred to 'officials knowledgeable in laws.' (Yukio Hisaki, ''Study of Ancient Japanese Schools'' (1990, Tamagawa University Press) ISBN 4-4720-7981-X).</ref>.
Initially, it was called '''Ritsugaku Hakase''' (律学博士), but after the establishment of {{ill|Meihōsei|ja|明法生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meihōsei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明法道|1=Myōbōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512427}} on {{ill|March 27 (lunar calendar)|ja|3月27日 (旧暦)|1=March 27 (Lunisolar)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 27 (lunar calendar)|qq=}} of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 2 (April 18, 730), it was soon renamed '''Myōhō Hakase'''. Under Myōhō Hakase were '''Myōhō Tokugyōshō''' (明法得業生) with 2 positions and '''Myōhōshō''' (明法生) with 10 positions (later increased to 20). Over time, one of the key duties was drafting and submitting {{ill|Meihō Kanbun|ja|明法勘文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meihō Kanbun|1=myōbō kanmon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512423}}, legal opinions during court discussions like {{ill|Jinden|ja|陣定|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinden|1=jinnosadame|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658177}}.
By the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the positions became hereditary among the {{ill|Sanuki clan|ja|讃岐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki clan|1=Sanuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929774}} and {{ill|Imomune clan|ja|惟宗氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imomune clan|1=Koremune clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492955}}, but during the medieval period, these were replaced by the renowned {{ill|Sakanoue clan|ja|坂上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue clan|1=Sakanoue clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10928477}} and {{ill|Nakahara clan|ja|中原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahara clan|1=Nakahara clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11363164}}<ref>In [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkū 2]] (1191), [[:en:Ōe no Hiromoto|Nakahara no Hiromoto]] was appointed as Myōhō Hakase through the recommendation of {{ill|Tsuchimikado no Michichika|ja|土御門通親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado no Michichika|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源通親|1=Minamoto no Michichika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564317}} while serving as the {{ill|Mandokoro Betto|ja|政所別当|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mandokoro Betto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura Shogunate]]. This appointment was harshly criticized by [[:en:Fujiwara no Kanezane|Kujō Kanezane]] as violating precedent, as Hiromoto was of the {{ill|Monjōdō|ja|明経道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monjōdō|1=myōgyōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11241198}} branch of the Nakahara family (who later adopted the Ōe surname). However, Hiromoto resigned on November 5 of that year. It is generally believed that this resignation was due to displeasure from [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] over free appointments, but another view suggests that Yoritomo had plans to place the {{ill|Office of Police and Justice|lt=Office of Police and Justice|lt=Kebiishi|ja|検非違使庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kebiishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 検非違使}} under the control of the Shogunate and appointed Hiromoto to oversee the legal aspects but made him resign after the {{ill|Kenkū 2 Uprising|ja|建久二年の強訴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkū 2 Uprising|1=Gōso of 1191|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486617}} could not be prevented (Tomohiro Saeki, ''Ichijō Yoshiyasu and Early Kamakura Public-Private Relations,'' ''Ancient Culture'' 564, 2006).</ref>.
== Notes == <references />
== Related Topics == * [[:en:Daigaku-ryō|Daigaku-ryō]] * {{ill|Meihō-dō|ja|明法道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meihō-dō|1=Myōbōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512427}} * {{ill|Seta Shōho|ja|勢多章甫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seta Shōho|1=Seta Norimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q84977839}} – The last Myōhō Hakase * [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] * {{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Ritsuryō law|ja|律令法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō law|qq=}} * [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryō]]

However, Hiromoto resigned on November 5 of that year. It is generally believed that this resignation was due to displeasure from [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] over free appointments, but another view suggests that Yoritomo had plans to place the {{ill|Office of Police and Justice|lt=Office of Police and Justice|lt=Kebiishi|ja|検非違使庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kebiishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 検非違使}} under the control of the Shogunate and appointed Hiromoto to oversee the legal aspects but made him resign after the {{ill|Kenkū 2 Uprising|ja|建久二年の強訴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkū 2 Uprising|1=Gōso of 1191|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486617}} could not be prevented (Tomohiro Saeki, ''Ichijō Yoshiyasu and Early Kamakura Public-Private Relations,'' ''Ancient Culture'' 564, 2006).</ref>.

[[ja:明法博士]] [[ja:%E6%98%8E%E6%B3%95%E5%8D%9A%E5%A3%AB]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:明法博士]] {{wikidata link|Q11512424}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Extraordinary Government Officials of Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Historical jurisprudence in Japan]] [[Category:Legal history of Japan]] [[Category:Ritsuryō System of Japan]] [[Category:University Dormitories]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:明法博士]][[zh:明法博士]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:明法博士]][[zh:明法博士]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:明法博士]][[zh:明法博士]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11512424]] Wikidata name: myōbō hakase [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox officeholder | name = '''Myōhō Hakase''' (明法博士) | native_name = 明法博士 | title = 明法博士 | department = [[Daigakuryō]] | type = [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] official | established = [[Shōmu]] Era, 728 CE | abolished = Medieval period | related_posts = [[Meihō-dō]] official, [[Ritsuryō]] scholar }}
'''Myōhō Hakase''' (明法博士, みょうほうはかせ) was an [[:en:Official|Official]] belonging to the [[:en:Daigaku-ryō|Daigaku-ryō]] under the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] in ancient Japan. It was an {{ill|Extraordinary Office|ja|令外官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Extraordinary Office|1=East Asian extra-statutory office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378315}} with two positions. Initially, it was Ranked as ''Shōshichii-ge'' (正七位下). Later, it came to be filled by members of prestigious families.
== History == Myōhō Hakase was established along with {{ill|Monjō Hakase|ja|文章博士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monjō Hakase|1=monjō hakase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500074}} under the {{ill|Kaku|ja|格式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaku|1=ge and shi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11536498}} issued on {{ill|July 21 (lunar calendar)|ja|7月21日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 21 (lunar calendar)|1=21st day of the 7th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q839764}} of [[:en:Jinki (era)|Shinki]] 5 (August 30, 728)<ref> [[:en:Shoku Nihongi|Shoku Nihongi]], entry for Daibō (8th day) of the 8th month of Taika Gannen. This record mentions dispatching Myōhō Hakase to various circuits (excluding Saikaidō) to explain the [[:en:Taihō Code|Taihō Ritsuryō]]. There are theories that Myōhō Hakase existed during the [[:en:Asuka Kiyomihara Code|Asuka Kiyomihara Ritsuryō]] period but was not established under the Taihō Ritsuryō, and that the term referred to 'officials knowledgeable in laws.' (Yukio Hisaki, ''Study of Ancient Japanese Schools'' (1990, Tamagawa University Press) ISBN 4-4720-7981-X).</ref>.
Initially, it was called '''Ritsugaku Hakase''' (律学博士), but after the establishment of {{ill|Meihōsei|ja|明法生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meihōsei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明法道|1=Myōbōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512427}} on {{ill|March 27 (lunar calendar)|ja|3月27日 (旧暦)|1=March 27 (Lunisolar)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 27 (lunar calendar)|qq=}} of [[:en:Tenpyō|Tenpyō]] 2 (April 18, 730), it was soon renamed '''Myōhō Hakase'''. Under Myōhō Hakase were '''Myōhō Tokugyōshō''' (明法得業生) with 2 positions and '''Myōhōshō''' (明法生) with 10 positions (later increased to 20). Over time, one of the key duties was drafting and submitting {{ill|Meihō Kanbun|ja|明法勘文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meihō Kanbun|1=myōbō kanmon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512423}}, legal opinions during court discussions like {{ill|Jinden|ja|陣定|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinden|1=jinnosadame|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658177}}.
By the mid-[[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], the positions became hereditary among the {{ill|Sanuki clan|ja|讃岐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki clan|1=Sanuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929774}} and {{ill|Imomune clan|ja|惟宗氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imomune clan|1=Koremune clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492955}}, but during the medieval period, these were replaced by the renowned {{ill|Sakanoue clan|ja|坂上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue clan|1=Sakanoue clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10928477}} and {{ill|Nakahara clan|ja|中原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahara clan|1=Nakahara clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11363164}}<ref>In [[:en:Kenkyū|Kenkū 2]] (1191), [[:en:Ōe no Hiromoto|Nakahara no Hiromoto]] was appointed as Myōhō Hakase through the recommendation of {{ill|Tsuchimikado no Michichika|ja|土御門通親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado no Michichika|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源通親|1=Minamoto no Michichika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564317}} while serving as the {{ill|Mandokoro Betto|ja|政所別当|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mandokoro Betto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the [[:en:Kamakura shogunate|Kamakura Shogunate]]. This appointment was harshly criticized by [[:en:Fujiwara no Kanezane|Kujō Kanezane]] as violating precedent, as Hiromoto was of the {{ill|Monjōdō|ja|明経道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monjōdō|1=myōgyōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11241198}} branch of the Nakahara family (who later adopted the Ōe surname). However, Hiromoto resigned on November 5 of that year. It is generally believed that this resignation was due to displeasure from [[:en:Minamoto no Yoritomo|Minamoto no Yoritomo]] over free appointments, but another view suggests that Yoritomo had plans to place the {{ill|Office of Police and Justice|lt=Office of Police and Justice|lt=Kebiishi|ja|検非違使庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kebiishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 検非違使}} under the control of the Shogunate and appointed Hiromoto to oversee the legal aspects but made him resign after the {{ill|Kenkū 2 Uprising|ja|建久二年の強訴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkū 2 Uprising|1=Gōso of 1191|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486617}} could not be prevented (Tomohiro Saeki, ''Ichijō Yoshiyasu and Early Kamakura Public-Private Relations,'' ''Ancient Culture'' 564, 2006).</ref>.
== Notes == <references />
== Related Topics == * [[:en:Daigaku-ryō|Daigaku-ryō]] * {{ill|Meihō-dō|ja|明法道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meihō-dō|1=Myōbōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512427}} * {{ill|Seta Shōho|ja|勢多章甫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seta Shōho|1=Seta Norimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q84977839}} – The last Myōhō Hakase * [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]] * {{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Ritsuryō law|ja|律令法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō law|qq=}} * [[:en:Traditional Chinese law|Ritsuryō]] {{translated page|ja|明法博士|version=91907183|comment=Imported full ja history}}

'''Myōhō Hakase''' (明法博士, みょうほうはかせ) was an {{ill|Official|en|Official|WD=Q599151|lt=Official}} belonging to the {{ill|Daigaku-ryō|en|Daigaku-ryō|WD=Q3012048|lt=Daigaku-ryō}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system}} in ancient Japan. It was an {{ill|Extraordinary Office|ja|令外官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Extraordinary Office|1=East Asian extra-statutory office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378315}} with two positions. Initially, it was Ranked as ''Shōshichii-ge'' (正七位下). Later, it came to be filled by members of prestigious families.
== History == Myōhō Hakase was established along with {{ill|Monjō Hakase|ja|文章博士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monjō Hakase|1=monjō hakase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500074}} under the {{ill|Kaku|ja|格式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaku|1=ge and shi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11536498}} issued on {{ill|July 21 (lunar calendar)|ja|7月21日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 21 (lunar calendar)|1=21st day of the 7th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q839764}} of {{ill|Jinki (era)|en|Jinki (era)|WD=Q1373210|lt=Shinki}} 5 (August 30, 728)<ref> {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi}}, entry for Daibō (8th day) of the 8th month of Taika Gannen. This record mentions dispatching Myōhō Hakase to various circuits (excluding Saikaidō) to explain the {{ill|Taihō Code|en|Taihō Code|WD=Q1059892|lt=Taihō Ritsuryō}}. There are theories that Myōhō Hakase existed during the {{ill|Asuka Kiyomihara Code|en|Asuka Kiyomihara Code|WD=Q2981422|lt=Asuka Kiyomihara Ritsuryō}} period but was not established under the Taihō Ritsuryō, and that the term referred to 'officials knowledgeable in laws.' (Yukio Hisaki, ''Study of Ancient Japanese Schools'' (1990, Tamagawa University Press) ISBN 4-4720-7981-X).</ref>.
Initially, it was called '''Ritsugaku Hakase''' (律学博士), but after the establishment of {{ill|Meihōsei|ja|明法生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meihōsei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明法道|1=Myōbōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512427}} on {{ill|March 27 (lunar calendar)|ja|3月27日 (旧暦)|1=March 27 (Lunisolar)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 27 (lunar calendar)|qq=}} of {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō}} 2 (April 18, 730), it was soon renamed '''Myōhō Hakase'''. Under Myōhō Hakase were '''Myōhō Tokugyōshō''' (明法得業生) with 2 positions and '''Myōhōshō''' (明法生) with 10 positions (later increased to 20). Over time, one of the key duties was drafting and submitting {{ill|Meihō Kanbun|ja|明法勘文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meihō Kanbun|1=myōbō kanmon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512423}}, legal opinions during court discussions like {{ill|Jinden|ja|陣定|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinden|1=jinnosadame|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658177}}.
By the mid-{{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, the positions became hereditary among the {{ill|Sanuki clan|ja|讃岐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki clan|1=Sanuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929774}} and {{ill|Imomune clan|ja|惟宗氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imomune clan|1=Koremune clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492955}}, but during the medieval period, these were replaced by the renowned {{ill|Sakanoue clan|ja|坂上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue clan|1=Sakanoue clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10928477}} and {{ill|Nakahara clan|ja|中原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahara clan|1=Nakahara clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11363164}}<ref>In {{ill|Kenkyū|en|Kenkyū|WD=Q1193933|lt=Kenkū 2}} (1191), {{ill|Ōe no Hiromoto|en|Ōe no Hiromoto|WD=Q384829|lt=Nakahara no Hiromoto}} was appointed as Myōhō Hakase through the recommendation of {{ill|Tsuchimikado no Michichika|ja|土御門通親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado no Michichika|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源通親|1=Minamoto no Michichika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564317}} while serving as the {{ill|Mandokoro Betto|ja|政所別当|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mandokoro Betto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|WD=Q736839|lt=Kamakura Shogunate}}. This appointment was harshly criticized by {{ill|Fujiwara no Kanezane|en|Fujiwara no Kanezane|WD=Q710214|lt=Kujō Kanezane}} as violating precedent, as Hiromoto was of the {{ill|Monjōdō|ja|明経道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monjōdō|1=myōgyōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11241198}} branch of the Nakahara family (who later adopted the Ōe surname). However, Hiromoto resigned on November 5 of that year. It is generally believed that this resignation was due to displeasure from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} over free appointments, but another view suggests that Yoritomo had plans to place the {{ill|Office of Police and Justice|lt=Office of Police and Justice|lt=Kebiishi|ja|検非違使庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kebiishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 検非違使}} under the control of the Shogunate and appointed Hiromoto to oversee the legal aspects but made him resign after the {{ill|Kenkū 2 Uprising|ja|建久二年の強訴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkū 2 Uprising|1=Gōso of 1191|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486617}} could not be prevented (Tomohiro Saeki, ''Ichijō Yoshiyasu and Early Kamakura Public-Private Relations,'' ''Ancient Culture'' 564, 2006).</ref>.
== Notes == <references />
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Daigaku-ryō|en|Daigaku-ryō|WD=Q3012048|lt=Daigaku-ryō}} * {{ill|Meihō-dō|ja|明法道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meihō-dō|1=Myōbōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512427}} * {{ill|Seta Shōho|ja|勢多章甫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seta Shōho|1=Seta Norimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q84977839}} – The last Myōhō Hakase * {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system}} * {{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Ritsuryō law|ja|律令法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō law|qq=}} * {{ill|Traditional Chinese law|en|Traditional Chinese law|WD=Q4380197|lt=Ritsuryō}} {{translated page|ja|明法博士|version=91907183|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:明法博士]] [[ja:%E6%98%8E%E6%B3%95%E5%8D%9A%E5%A3%AB]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:明法博士]] {{wikidata link|Q11512424}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Extraordinary Government Officials of Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Historical jurisprudence in Japan]] [[Category:Legal history of Japan]] [[Category:Ritsuryō System of Japan]] [[Category:University Dormitories]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
