revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:明法博士]][[zh:明法博士]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:明法博士]][[zh:明法博士]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:明法博士]][[zh:明法博士]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11512424]] Wikidata name: myōbō hakase [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:22, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox officeholder | name = '''Myōhō Hakase''' (明法博士) | native_name = 明法博士 | title = 明法博士 | department = [[Daigakuryō]] | type = {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system}} official | established = [[Shōmu]] Era, 728 CE | abolished = Medieval period | related_posts = [[Meihō-dō]] official, [[Ritsuryō]] scholar }}
'''Myōhō Hakase''' (明法博士, みょうほうはかせ) was an {{ill|Official|en|Official|WD=Q599151|lt=Official}} belonging to the {{ill|Daigaku-ryō|en|Daigaku-ryō|WD=Q3012048|lt=Daigaku-ryō}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system}} in ancient Japan. It was an {{ill|Extraordinary Office|ja|令外官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Extraordinary Office|1=East Asian extra-statutory office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378315}} with two positions. Initially, it was Ranked as ''Shōshichii-ge'' (正七位下). Later, it came to be filled by members of prestigious families.
== History == Myōhō Hakase was established along with {{ill|Monjō Hakase|ja|文章博士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monjō Hakase|1=monjō hakase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500074}} under the {{ill|Kaku|ja|格式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaku|1=ge and shi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11536498}} issued on {{ill|July 21 (lunar calendar)|ja|7月21日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 21 (lunar calendar)|1=21st day of the 7th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q839764}} of {{ill|Jinki (era)|en|Jinki (era)|WD=Q1373210|lt=Shinki}} 5 (August 30, 728)<ref> {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi}}, entry for Daibō (8th day) of the 8th month of Taika Gannen. This record mentions dispatching Myōhō Hakase to various circuits (excluding Saikaidō) to explain the {{ill|Taihō Code|en|Taihō Code|WD=Q1059892|lt=Taihō Ritsuryō}}. There are theories that Myōhō Hakase existed during the {{ill|Asuka Kiyomihara Code|en|Asuka Kiyomihara Code|WD=Q2981422|lt=Asuka Kiyomihara Ritsuryō}} period but was not established under the Taihō Ritsuryō, and that the term referred to 'officials knowledgeable in laws.' (Yukio Hisaki, ''Study of Ancient Japanese Schools'' (1990, Tamagawa University Press) ISBN 4-4720-7981-X).</ref>.
Initially, it was called '''Ritsugaku Hakase''' (律学博士), but after the establishment of {{ill|Meihōsei|ja|明法生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meihōsei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明法道|1=Myōbōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512427}} on {{ill|March 27 (lunar calendar)|ja|3月27日 (旧暦)|1=March 27 (Lunisolar)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 27 (lunar calendar)|qq=}} of {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō}} 2 (April 18, 730), it was soon renamed '''Myōhō Hakase'''. Under Myōhō Hakase were '''Myōhō Tokugyōshō''' (明法得業生) with 2 positions and '''Myōhōshō''' (明法生) with 10 positions (later increased to 20). Over time, one of the key duties was drafting and submitting {{ill|Meihō Kanbun|ja|明法勘文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meihō Kanbun|1=myōbō kanmon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512423}}, legal opinions during court discussions like {{ill|Jinden|ja|陣定|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinden|1=jinnosadame|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658177}}.
By the mid-{{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, the positions became hereditary among the {{ill|Sanuki clan|ja|讃岐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki clan|1=Sanuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929774}} and {{ill|Imomune clan|ja|惟宗氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imomune clan|1=Koremune clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492955}}, but during the medieval period, these were replaced by the renowned {{ill|Sakanoue clan|ja|坂上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue clan|1=Sakanoue clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10928477}} and {{ill|Nakahara clan|ja|中原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahara clan|1=Nakahara clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11363164}}<ref>In {{ill|Kenkyū|en|Kenkyū|WD=Q1193933|lt=Kenkū 2}} (1191), {{ill|Ōe no Hiromoto|en|Ōe no Hiromoto|WD=Q384829|lt=Nakahara no Hiromoto}} was appointed as Myōhō Hakase through the recommendation of {{ill|Tsuchimikado no Michichika|ja|土御門通親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado no Michichika|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源通親|1=Minamoto no Michichika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564317}} while serving as the {{ill|Mandokoro Betto|ja|政所別当|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mandokoro Betto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|WD=Q736839|lt=Kamakura Shogunate}}. This appointment was harshly criticized by {{ill|Fujiwara no Kanezane|en|Fujiwara no Kanezane|WD=Q710214|lt=Kujō Kanezane}} as violating precedent, as Hiromoto was of the {{ill|Monjōdō|ja|明経道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monjōdō|1=myōgyōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11241198}} branch of the Nakahara family (who later adopted the Ōe surname). However, Hiromoto resigned on November 5 of that year. It is generally believed that this resignation was due to displeasure from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} over free appointments, but another view suggests that Yoritomo had plans to place the {{ill|Office of Police and Justice|lt=Office of Police and Justice|lt=Kebiishi|ja|検非違使庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kebiishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 検非違使}} under the control of the Shogunate and appointed Hiromoto to oversee the legal aspects but made him resign after the {{ill|Kenkū 2 Uprising|ja|建久二年の強訴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkū 2 Uprising|1=Gōso of 1191|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486617}} could not be prevented (Tomohiro Saeki, ''Ichijō Yoshiyasu and Early Kamakura Public-Private Relations,'' ''Ancient Culture'' 564, 2006).</ref>.
== Notes == <references />
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Daigaku-ryō|en|Daigaku-ryō|WD=Q3012048|lt=Daigaku-ryō}} * {{ill|Meihō-dō|ja|明法道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meihō-dō|1=Myōbōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512427}} * {{ill|Seta Shōho|ja|勢多章甫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seta Shōho|1=Seta Norimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q84977839}} – The last Myōhō Hakase * {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system}} * {{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Ritsuryō law|ja|律令法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō law|qq=}} * {{ill|Traditional Chinese law|en|Traditional Chinese law|WD=Q4380197|lt=Ritsuryō}} {{translated page|ja|明法博士|version=91907183|comment=Imported full ja history}}

It was an {{ill|Extraordinary Office|ja|令外官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Extraordinary Office|1=East Asian extra-statutory office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378315}} with two positions. Initially, it was Ranked as ''Shōshichii-ge'' (正七位下). Later, it came to be filled by members of prestigious families.
== History == Myōhō Hakase was established along with {{ill|Monjō Hakase|ja|文章博士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monjō Hakase|1=monjō hakase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500074}} under the {{ill|Kaku|ja|格式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaku|1=ge and shi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11536498}} issued on {{ill|July 21 (lunar calendar)|ja|7月21日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 21 (lunar calendar)|1=21st day of the 7th month in the Chinese calendar|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q839764}} of {{ill|Jinki (era)|en|Jinki (era)|WD=Q1373210|lt=Shinki}} 5 (August 30, 728)<ref> {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi}}, entry for Daibō (8th day) of the 8th month of Taika Gannen. This record mentions dispatching Myōhō Hakase to various circuits (excluding Saikaidō) to explain the {{ill|Taihō Code|en|Taihō Code|WD=Q1059892|lt=Taihō Ritsuryō}}. There are theories that Myōhō Hakase existed during the {{ill|Asuka Kiyomihara Code|en|Asuka Kiyomihara Code|WD=Q2981422|lt=Asuka Kiyomihara Ritsuryō}} period but was not established under the Taihō Ritsuryō, and that the term referred to 'officials knowledgeable in laws.' (Yukio Hisaki, ''Study of Ancient Japanese Schools'' (1990, Tamagawa University Press) ISBN 4-4720-7981-X).</ref>.
Initially, it was called '''Ritsugaku Hakase''' (律学博士), but after the establishment of {{ill|Meihōsei|ja|明法生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meihōsei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明法道|1=Myōbōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512427}} on {{ill|March 27 (lunar calendar)|ja|3月27日 (旧暦)|1=March 27 (Lunisolar)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 27 (lunar calendar)|qq=}} of {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō}} 2 (April 18, 730), it was soon renamed '''Myōhō Hakase'''. Under Myōhō Hakase were '''Myōhō Tokugyōshō''' (明法得業生) with 2 positions and '''Myōhōshō''' (明法生) with 10 positions (later increased to 20). Over time, one of the key duties was drafting and submitting {{ill|Meihō Kanbun|ja|明法勘文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meihō Kanbun|1=myōbō kanmon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512423}}, legal opinions during court discussions like {{ill|Jinden|ja|陣定|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinden|1=jinnosadame|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658177}}.
By the mid-{{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period}}, the positions became hereditary among the {{ill|Sanuki clan|ja|讃岐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki clan|1=Sanuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929774}} and {{ill|Imomune clan|ja|惟宗氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imomune clan|1=Koremune clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492955}}, but during the medieval period, these were replaced by the renowned {{ill|Sakanoue clan|ja|坂上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue clan|1=Sakanoue clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10928477}} and {{ill|Nakahara clan|ja|中原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahara clan|1=Nakahara clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11363164}}<ref>In {{ill|Kenkyū|en|Kenkyū|WD=Q1193933|lt=Kenkū 2}} (1191), {{ill|Ōe no Hiromoto|en|Ōe no Hiromoto|WD=Q384829|lt=Nakahara no Hiromoto}} was appointed as Myōhō Hakase through the recommendation of {{ill|Tsuchimikado no Michichika|ja|土御門通親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado no Michichika|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源通親|1=Minamoto no Michichika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564317}} while serving as the {{ill|Mandokoro Betto|ja|政所別当|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mandokoro Betto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|WD=Q736839|lt=Kamakura Shogunate}}. This appointment was harshly criticized by {{ill|Fujiwara no Kanezane|en|Fujiwara no Kanezane|WD=Q710214|lt=Kujō Kanezane}} as violating precedent, as Hiromoto was of the {{ill|Monjōdō|ja|明経道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monjōdō|1=myōgyōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11241198}} branch of the Nakahara family (who later adopted the Ōe surname). However, Hiromoto resigned on November 5 of that year. It is generally believed that this resignation was due to displeasure from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} over free appointments, but another view suggests that Yoritomo had plans to place the {{ill|Office of Police and Justice|lt=Office of Police and Justice|lt=Kebiishi|ja|検非違使庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kebiishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 検非違使}} under the control of the Shogunate and appointed Hiromoto to oversee the legal aspects but made him resign after the {{ill|Kenkū 2 Uprising|ja|建久二年の強訴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkū 2 Uprising|1=Gōso of 1191|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486617}} could not be prevented (Tomohiro Saeki, ''Ichijō Yoshiyasu and Early Kamakura Public-Private Relations,'' ''Ancient Culture'' 564, 2006).</ref>.
== Notes == <references />
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Daigaku-ryō|en|Daigaku-ryō|WD=Q3012048|lt=Daigaku-ryō}} * {{ill|Meihō-dō|ja|明法道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meihō-dō|1=Myōbōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512427}} * {{ill|Seta Shōho|ja|勢多章甫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seta Shōho|1=Seta Norimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q84977839}} – The last Myōhō Hakase * {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system}} * {{ill|Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Kyaku and Shiki|lt=Ritsuryō law|ja|律令法|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ritsuryō law|qq=}} * {{ill|Traditional Chinese law|en|Traditional Chinese law|WD=Q4380197|lt=Ritsuryō}} {{translated page|ja|明法博士|version=91907183|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Initially, it was Ranked as ''Shōshichii-ge'' (正七位下). Later, it came to be filled by members of prestigious families.

There are theories that Myōhō Hakase existed during the {{ill|Asuka Kiyomihara Code|en|Asuka Kiyomihara Code|WD=Q2981422|lt=Asuka Kiyomihara Ritsuryō}} period but was not established under the Taihō Ritsuryō, and that the term referred to 'officials knowledgeable in laws.' (Yukio Hisaki, ''Study of Ancient Japanese Schools'' (1990, Tamagawa University Press) ISBN 4-4720-7981-X).</ref>.

Over time, one of the key duties was drafting and submitting {{ill|Meihō Kanbun|ja|明法勘文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meihō Kanbun|1=myōbō kanmon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512423}}, legal opinions during court discussions like {{ill|Jinden|ja|陣定|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinden|1=jinnosadame|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658177}}.

It is generally believed that this resignation was due to displeasure from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} over free appointments, but another view suggests that Yoritomo had plans to place the {{ill|Office of Police and Justice|lt=Office of Police and Justice|lt=Kebiishi|ja|検非違使庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kebiishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 検非違使}} under the control of the Shogunate and appointed Hiromoto to oversee the legal aspects but made him resign after the {{ill|Kenkū 2 Uprising|ja|建久二年の強訴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkū 2 Uprising|1=Gōso of 1191|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486617}} could not be prevented (Tomohiro Saeki, ''Ichijō Yoshiyasu and Early Kamakura Public-Private Relations,'' ''Ancient Culture'' 564, 2006).</ref>.

[[ja:明法博士]] [[ja:%E6%98%8E%E6%B3%95%E5%8D%9A%E5%A3%AB]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[zh:明法博士]] {{wikidata link|Q11512424}} [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Extraordinary Government Officials of Ancient Japan]] [[Category:Historical jurisprudence in Japan]] [[Category:Legal history of Japan]] [[Category:Ritsuryō System of Japan]] [[Category:University Dormitories]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:明法博士]][[zh:明法博士]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:明法博士]][[zh:明法博士]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:明法博士]][[zh:明法博士]]
{{Infobox officeholder | name = '''Myōhō Hakase''' (明法博士) | native_name = 明法博士 | title = 明法博士 | department = [[Daigakuryō]] | type = {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}} official | established = [[Shōmu]] Era, 728 CE | abolished = Medieval period | related_posts = [[Meihō-dō]] official, [[Ritsuryō]] scholar }}
'''Myōhō Hakase''' (明法博士, みょうほうはかせ) was an {{ill|Official|en|Official|WD=Q599151|lt=Official|1=Official}} belonging to the {{ill|Daigaku-ryō|en|Daigaku-ryō|WD=Q3012048|lt=Daigaku-ryō|1=Daigaku-ryō}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}} in ancient Japan. It was an {{ill|Extraordinary Office|ja|令外官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Extraordinary Office|1=East Asian extra-statutory office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11378315}} with two positions. Initially, it was Ranked as ''Shōshichii-ge'' (正七位下). Later, it came to be filled by members of prestigious families.
== History == Myōhō Hakase was established along with {{ill|Monjō Hakase|ja|文章博士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monjō Hakase|1=monjō hakase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11500074}} under the {{ill|Kaku|ja|格式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaku|1=Kyaku and Shiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11536498}} issued on {{ill|July 21 (lunar calendar)|ja|7月21日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 21 (lunar calendar)|1=July 21 (Lunisolar)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q839764}} of {{ill|Jinki (era)|en|Jinki (era)|WD=Q1373210|lt=Shinki|1=Jinki (era)}} 5 (August 30, 728)<ref> {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|WD=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihongi}}, entry for Daibō (8th day) of the 8th month of Taika Gannen. This record mentions dispatching Myōhō Hakase to various circuits (excluding Saikaidō) to explain the {{ill|Taihō Code|en|Taihō Code|WD=Q1059892|lt=Taihō Ritsuryō|1=Taihō Code}}. There are theories that Myōhō Hakase existed during the {{ill|Asuka Kiyomihara Code|en|Asuka Kiyomihara Code|WD=Q2981422|lt=Asuka Kiyomihara Ritsuryō|1=Asuka Kiyomihara Code}} period but was not established under the Taihō Ritsuryō, and that the term referred to 'officials knowledgeable in laws.' (Yukio Hisaki, ''Study of Ancient Japanese Schools'' (1990, Tamagawa University Press) ISBN 4-4720-7981-X).</ref>.
Initially, it was called '''Ritsugaku Hakase''' (律学博士), but after the establishment of {{ill|Meihōsei|ja|明法生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meihōsei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明法道|1=Myōbōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512427}} on {{ill|March 27 (lunar calendar)|ja|3月27日 (旧暦)|1=March 27 (Lunisolar)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 27 (lunar calendar)|qq=}} of {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|WD=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō|1=Tenpyō}} 2 (April 18, 730), it was soon renamed '''Myōhō Hakase'''. Under Myōhō Hakase were '''Myōhō Tokugyōshō''' (明法得業生) with 2 positions and '''Myōhōshō''' (明法生) with 10 positions (later increased to 20). Over time, one of the key duties was drafting and submitting {{ill|Meihō Kanbun|ja|明法勘文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meihō Kanbun|1=myōbō kanmon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11512423}}, legal opinions during court discussions like {{ill|Jinden|ja|陣定|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinden|1=jinnosadame|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11658177}}.
By the mid-{{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|WD=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, the positions became hereditary among the {{ill|Sanuki clan|ja|讃岐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki clan|1=Sanuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q30929774}} and {{ill|Imomune clan|ja|惟宗氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imomune clan|1=Koremune clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11492955}}, but during the medieval period, these were replaced by the renowned {{ill|Sakanoue clan|ja|坂上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue clan|1=Sakanoue clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10928477}} and {{ill|Nakahara clan|ja|中原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahara clan|1=Nakahara clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11363164}}<ref>In {{ill|Kenkyū|en|Kenkyū|WD=Q1193933|lt=Kenkū 2|1=Kenkyū}} (1191), {{ill|Ōe no Hiromoto|en|Ōe no Hiromoto|WD=Q384829|lt=Nakahara no Hiromoto|1=Ōe no Hiromoto}} was appointed as Myōhō Hakase through the recommendation of {{ill|Tsuchimikado no Michichika|ja|土御門通親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado no Michichika|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源通親|1=Minamoto no Michichika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11564317}} while serving as the {{ill|Mandokoro Betto|ja|政所別当|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mandokoro Betto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|WD=Q736839|lt=Kamakura Shogunate|1=Kamakura shogunate}}. This appointment was harshly criticized by {{ill|Fujiwara no Kanezane|en|Fujiwara no Kanezane|WD=Q710214|lt=Kujō Kanezane|1=Fujiwara no Kanezane}} as violating precedent, as Hiromoto was of the {{ill|Monjōdō|ja|明経道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monjōdō|1=myōgyōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11241198}} branch of the Nakahara family (who later adopted the Ōe surname). However, Hiromoto resigned on November 5 of that year. It is generally believed that this resignation was due to displeasure from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} over free appointments, but another view suggests that Yoritomo had plans to place the {{ill|Office of Police and Justice|lt=Office of Police and Justice|lt=Kebiishi|ja|検非違使庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kebiishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 検非違使}} under the control of the Shogunate and appointed Hiromoto to oversee the legal aspects but made him resign after the {{ill|Kenkū 2 Uprising|ja|建久二年の強訴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkū 2 Uprising|1=Gōso of 1191|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11486617}} could not be prevented (Tomohiro Saeki, ''Ichijō Yoshiyasu and Early Kamakura Public-Private Relations,'' ''Ancient Culture'' 564, 2006).</ref>.

Initially, it was called '''Ritsugaku Hakase''' (律学博士), but after the establishment of {{ill|Meihōsei|ja|明法生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meihōsei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明法道|1=Myōbōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11512427}} on {{ill|March 27 (lunar calendar)|ja|3月27日 (旧暦)|1=March 27 (Lunisolar)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 27 (lunar calendar)|qq=}} of {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|qid=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō|1=Tenpyō}} 2 (April 18, 730), it was soon renamed '''Myōhō Hakase'''. Under Myōhō Hakase were '''Myōhō Tokugyōshō''' (明法得業生) with 2 positions and '''Myōhōshō''' (明法生) with 10 positions (later increased to 20). Over time, one of the key duties was drafting and submitting {{ill|Meihō Kanbun|ja|明法勘文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meihō Kanbun|1=myōbō kanmon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11512423}}, legal opinions during court discussions like {{ill|Jinden|ja|陣定|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinden|1=jinnosadame|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11658177}}.
By the mid-{{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, the positions became hereditary among the {{ill|Sanuki clan|ja|讃岐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki clan|1=Sanuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q30929774}} and {{ill|Imomune clan|ja|惟宗氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imomune clan|1=Koremune clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11492955}}, but during the medieval period, these were replaced by the renowned {{ill|Sakanoue clan|ja|坂上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue clan|1=Sakanoue clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10928477}} and {{ill|Nakahara clan|ja|中原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahara clan|1=Nakahara clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11363164}}<ref>In {{ill|Kenkyū|en|Kenkyū|qid=Q1193933|lt=Kenkū 2|1=Kenkyū}} (1191), {{ill|Ōe no Hiromoto|en|Ōe no Hiromoto|qid=Q384829|lt=Nakahara no Hiromoto|1=Ōe no Hiromoto}} was appointed as Myōhō Hakase through the recommendation of {{ill|Tsuchimikado no Michichika|ja|土御門通親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado no Michichika|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源通親|1=Minamoto no Michichika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11564317}} while serving as the {{ill|Mandokoro Betto|ja|政所別当|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mandokoro Betto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|qid=Q736839|lt=Kamakura Shogunate|1=Kamakura shogunate}}. This appointment was harshly criticized by {{ill|Fujiwara no Kanezane|en|Fujiwara no Kanezane|qid=Q710214|lt=Kujō Kanezane|1=Fujiwara no Kanezane}} as violating precedent, as Hiromoto was of the {{ill|Monjōdō|ja|明経道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monjōdō|1=myōgyōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11241198}} branch of the Nakahara family (who later adopted the Ōe surname). However, Hiromoto resigned on November 5 of that year. It is generally believed that this resignation was due to displeasure from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|qid=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} over free appointments, but another view suggests that Yoritomo had plans to place the {{ill|Office of Police and Justice|lt=Office of Police and Justice|lt=Kebiishi|ja|検非違使庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kebiishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 検非違使}} under the control of the Shogunate and appointed Hiromoto to oversee the legal aspects but made him resign after the {{ill|Kenkū 2 Uprising|ja|建久二年の強訴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkū 2 Uprising|1=Gōso of 1191|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11486617}} could not be prevented (Tomohiro Saeki, ''Ichijō Yoshiyasu and Early Kamakura Public-Private Relations,'' ''Ancient Culture'' 564, 2006).</ref>.
== Notes == <references />

revision.text:
{{Infobox officeholder | name = '''Myōhō Hakase''' (明法博士) | native_name = 明法博士 | title = 明法博士 | department = [[Daigakuryō]] | type = {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|qid=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}} official | established = [[Shōmu]] Era, 728 CE | abolished = Medieval period | related_posts = [[Meihō-dō]] official, [[Ritsuryō]] scholar }}
'''Myōhō Hakase''' (明法博士, みょうほうはかせ) was an {{ill|Official|en|Official|qid=Q599151|lt=Official|1=Official}} belonging to the {{ill|Daigaku-ryō|en|Daigaku-ryō|qid=Q3012048|lt=Daigaku-ryō|1=Daigaku-ryō}} under the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|qid=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}} in ancient Japan. It was an {{ill|Extraordinary Office|ja|令外官|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Extraordinary Office|1=East Asian extra-statutory office|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11378315}} with two positions. Initially, it was Ranked as ''Shōshichii-ge'' (正七位下). Later, it came to be filled by members of prestigious families.
== History == Myōhō Hakase was established along with {{ill|Monjō Hakase|ja|文章博士|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monjō Hakase|1=monjō hakase|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11500074}} under the {{ill|Kaku|ja|格式|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kaku|1=Kyaku and Shiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11536498}} issued on {{ill|July 21 (lunar calendar)|ja|7月21日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/July 21 (lunar calendar)|1=July 21 (Lunisolar)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q839764}} of {{ill|Jinki (era)|en|Jinki (era)|qid=Q1373210|lt=Shinki|1=Jinki (era)}} 5 (August 30, 728)<ref> {{ill|Shoku Nihongi|en|Shoku Nihongi|qid=Q611399|lt=Shoku Nihongi|1=Shoku Nihongi}}, entry for Daibō (8th day) of the 8th month of Taika Gannen. This record mentions dispatching Myōhō Hakase to various circuits (excluding Saikaidō) to explain the {{ill|Taihō Code|en|Taihō Code|qid=Q1059892|lt=Taihō Ritsuryō|1=Taihō Code}}. There are theories that Myōhō Hakase existed during the {{ill|Asuka Kiyomihara Code|en|Asuka Kiyomihara Code|qid=Q2981422|lt=Asuka Kiyomihara Ritsuryō|1=Asuka Kiyomihara Code}} period but was not established under the Taihō Ritsuryō, and that the term referred to 'officials knowledgeable in laws.' (Yukio Hisaki, ''Study of Ancient Japanese Schools'' (1990, Tamagawa University Press) ISBN 4-4720-7981-X).</ref>.
Initially, it was called '''Ritsugaku Hakase''' (律学博士), but after the establishment of {{ill|Meihōsei|ja|明法生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meihōsei|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 明法道|1=Myōbōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11512427}} on {{ill|March 27 (lunar calendar)|ja|3月27日 (旧暦)|1=March 27 (Lunisolar)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/March 27 (lunar calendar)|qq=}} of {{ill|Tenpyō|en|Tenpyō|qid=Q1199879|lt=Tenpyō|1=Tenpyō}} 2 (April 18, 730), it was soon renamed '''Myōhō Hakase'''. Under Myōhō Hakase were '''Myōhō Tokugyōshō''' (明法得業生) with 2 positions and '''Myōhōshō''' (明法生) with 10 positions (later increased to 20). Over time, one of the key duties was drafting and submitting {{ill|Meihō Kanbun|ja|明法勘文|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meihō Kanbun|1=myōbō kanmon|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11512423}}, legal opinions during court discussions like {{ill|Jinden|ja|陣定|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinden|1=jinnosadame|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11658177}}.
By the mid-{{ill|Heian period|en|Heian period|qid=Q193292|lt=Heian period|1=Heian period}}, the positions became hereditary among the {{ill|Sanuki clan|ja|讃岐氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sanuki clan|1=Sanuki clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q30929774}} and {{ill|Imomune clan|ja|惟宗氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Imomune clan|1=Koremune clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11492955}}, but during the medieval period, these were replaced by the renowned {{ill|Sakanoue clan|ja|坂上氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sakanoue clan|1=Sakanoue clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10928477}} and {{ill|Nakahara clan|ja|中原氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakahara clan|1=Nakahara clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11363164}}<ref>In {{ill|Kenkyū|en|Kenkyū|qid=Q1193933|lt=Kenkū 2|1=Kenkyū}} (1191), {{ill|Ōe no Hiromoto|en|Ōe no Hiromoto|qid=Q384829|lt=Nakahara no Hiromoto|1=Ōe no Hiromoto}} was appointed as Myōhō Hakase through the recommendation of {{ill|Tsuchimikado no Michichika|ja|土御門通親|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuchimikado no Michichika|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 源通親|1=Minamoto no Michichika|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11564317}} while serving as the {{ill|Mandokoro Betto|ja|政所別当|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mandokoro Betto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of the {{ill|Kamakura shogunate|en|Kamakura shogunate|qid=Q736839|lt=Kamakura Shogunate|1=Kamakura shogunate}}. This appointment was harshly criticized by {{ill|Fujiwara no Kanezane|en|Fujiwara no Kanezane|qid=Q710214|lt=Kujō Kanezane|1=Fujiwara no Kanezane}} as violating precedent, as Hiromoto was of the {{ill|Monjōdō|ja|明経道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Monjōdō|1=myōgyōdō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11241198}} branch of the Nakahara family (who later adopted the Ōe surname). However, Hiromoto resigned on November 5 of that year. It is generally believed that this resignation was due to displeasure from {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|qid=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}} over free appointments, but another view suggests that Yoritomo had plans to place the {{ill|Office of Police and Justice|lt=Office of Police and Justice|lt=Kebiishi|ja|検非違使庁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kebiishi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 検非違使}} under the control of the Shogunate and appointed Hiromoto to oversee the legal aspects but made him resign after the {{ill|Kenkū 2 Uprising|ja|建久二年の強訴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kenkū 2 Uprising|1=Gōso of 1191|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11486617}} could not be prevented (Tomohiro Saeki, ''Ichijō Yoshiyasu and Early Kamakura Public-Private Relations,'' ''Ancient Culture'' 564, 2006).</ref>.
